## vvx
Job Jockey Kit - MCP tools for project initiative management

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

7167b795  ₢BBAAs    Sweep rbw-* zipper channels from "" to "param1" and refactor handlers to read BUZ_FOLIO for the 14 enrollments whose orchestrator/operator-facing call shape passes a positional folio (vessel/hallmark/locator/stamp). Empirical post-BBAAs onboarding-sequence run (5/8 PASSED) hit `rbfd_enshrine: Vessel argument required (sigil or path)` on ordain-airgap's enshrine of rbev-bottle-ifrit-forge, with the diagnostic `WARNING: Colophon 'rbw-dE' takes no folio; ignoring unexpected argument(s): rbev-bottle-ifrit-forge` confirming the dispatcher stripped the arg before rbfd_enshrine's `${1:-}` could read it. Root cause: concurrent commit 17b3f85e (₢A-AAj, landed 2026-05-02 12:28:38 mid-fixture-run) tightened buz_exec_lookup's empty-channel branch from silent-pass-through to warn-and-clear ("Friendly-error UX extension: empty-channel commands now warn-and-clear when given unexpected positional args instead of silently passing them through to the formulary") to surface bogus args to truly-no-folio commands like buw-rnl. The change was correct in intent but unsynchronized: many rbw-* enrollments declared channel `""` while their handlers in rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh, rbfl_FoundryLedger.sh, rbfr_FoundryRetriever.sh, and rbfc_FoundryCore.sh read `${1:-}` for a folio they had been receiving via the prior silent-pass-through. ordain-conjure (case 4) passed because it ran at 12:03:45 — pre-17b3f85e — under the old behavior; enshrine-upstream ran at 12:30:13 — post-17b3f85e — under the new behavior and died on empty `${1:-}`. Fix shape mirrors the prior yoke fix (commit 4097610f, BBAAs): change channel `""` to `"param1"` in zipper enrollment, change handler's folio read from `${1:-}` to `${BUZ_FOLIO:-}`, and (for handlers with positional --force flag) shift the flag read from `${2:-}` to `${1:-}` since the dispatcher now consumes the folio and forwards remaining args. Affected colophons (orchestrator-passes-args set, identified via grep of rbtdri_invoke_global call sites in Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrc_crucible.rs and rbtdro_onboarding.rs): rbw-dE (rbfd_enshrine, vessel), rbw-fO (rbfd_ordain, vessel), rbw-fk (rbfd_kludge, vessel), rbw-fA (rbfl_abjure, hallmark + --force), rbw-fs (rbfr_summon, hallmark), rbw-fpf/rbw-fpc (rbfc_plumb_full/compact, hallmark), rbw-irh/rbw-irr (rbfl_rekon_hallmark/reliquary, hallmark/stamp), rbw-iwh/rbw-iwr/rbw-iwe (rbfl_wrest, locator), rbw-iJh/rbw-iJr/rbw-iJe (rbfl_jettison, locator + --force) — 14 enrollments, 11 distinct handlers (3 wrest + 3 jettison enrollments share their handlers). Excluded from sweep (genuinely no-folio, channel `""` correct): rbw-aM mantle, rbw-arr/rbw-adr roster, rbw-dL/dU/dl depot ops, rbw-dI inscribe, rbw-gPI/PE/PR/q payor, rbw-Occ/Ocr/Ocd/Ofc/Ots/Odf/Oda/Odb/Odg/Op/Og/o onboarding handbooks, rbw-iah/iar/iae audit, rbw-MZ/MP/MG marshal, rbw-ft tally, rbw-fV vouch — these handlers take no folio and the new warn-and-clear behavior catches operator typos correctly. BCG note on furnish: unlike the rbfl_yoke fix which moved zrbrv_kindle to rbfl_cli.sh's furnish per BCG "kindle is CLI-only" rule (yoke needed to write to a kindle constant RBRV_RELIQUARY before validation), the handlers in this sweep call `zrbfc_resolve_vessel`/`zrbfc_load_vessel` (pure source, no kindle) or consume hallmark/locator/stamp strings (no regime state at all), so reading BUZ_FOLIO from the function body is BCG-conformant and no CLI furnish changes are needed in rbfd_cli.sh, rbfl_cli.sh, rbfr_cli.sh, or rbfc_cli.sh. The dispatcher's `BUZ_FOLIO="${z_folio}" exec ...` (buz_zipper.sh:280) makes the folio available as an inherited environment variable to the function body. Tabtarget context regenerated via tt/rbw-MG.MarshalGenerate.sh — the Folio column for the 14 affected rows flips from `—` (empty channel) to `param1`, satisfying the qualify-fast freshness gate. Verification: bash -n green on all 5 edited shell files; tt/rbtd-b.Build.sh green (theurge dev profile); tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh 25/26 with rbtdrf_rv_rbrv_all_vessels pre-existing fail unchanged from BBAA3/BBAA4/BBAAs baseline (carrying-forward marshal-zero validator surfacing); tt/rbw-tf.QualifyFast.sh passes — tabtarget structure, RBW colophon registrations, and generated context freshness all green. No new shellcheck warnings expected (pattern-mechanical refactor, no new variable patterns or constructs introduced). Empirical re-run of onboarding-sequence pending operator go-ahead per docket iteration loop. Caveat: this sweep covers the orchestrator-passes-args set; if a handler outside that set (e.g., bash-level callers in handbook prose using positional args to a `""`-channel tabtarget) was relying on the old silent-pass-through, it will now warn-and-clear and fail downstream. Discovery recipe for residual sites: grep for `${1:-}` reads in rbg*/rbf*/rbh*/rbl*/rbr*/rbo*/rb[a-z]_*.sh handler functions and cross-reference against zipper channel column for `""`. Out of scope for this notch (deferred per docket): full project-wide audit of every `""`-channel handler's `${1:-}`/`${2:-}` reads to flush remaining latent failures before they surface empirically.

48abbe1e  ₢BBAAs    fix off-by-one in zrbfd_enshrine_extract_anchors after BBAA3 preflight prepended buildStepOutputs[0]. Empirical post-curl-primitive onboarding-sequence run (5/6 PASSED before stop) hit `[rbfd_enshrine] ERROR: Empty decoded buildStepOutputs` on ordain-airgap's enshrine of rbev-bottle-ifrit-forge. The Cloud Build itself was fine — submitted, queued 8 polls, working 3 polls, SUCCESS at poll 12, 19s wall clock; build 74cd601d-962c-41d1-8961-7483ae3967bf in canc-d-canest2100003. Direct gcloud describe of the build's results.buildStepOutputs revealed ["", "<base64>"] — slot 0 empty, slot 1 the actual enshrine-copy JSON {"slot_1":{"anchor":"rbi_es/rust-slim-bookworm-caaf9ca7ac:rust-slim-bookworm-caaf9ca7ac","origin":"rust:slim-bookworm","digest":"sha256:caaf9ca7ac..."}}. Root cause: BBAA3 (dca68895) prepended a step 0 'reliquary-preflight' to every ordain-path CB job; preflight does HEAD checks only and writes nothing to /builder/outputs/output, so its buildStepOutputs entry is empty string. The host-side enshrine extractor at rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh:1040 was written pre-BBAA3 when enshrine-copy was step 0 and reads buildStepOutputs[0]: jq -r '.results.buildStepOutputs[0] // empty' followed by base64 decode and `test -s` on the decoded file — which now decodes empty-string-base64 to empty-file and trips the 'Empty decoded buildStepOutputs' die at line 1046. Asymmetric evidence: the parallel conjure parser at rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh:1264 already reads buildStepOutputs[1] (conjure has always had a step preceding its hallmark step); BBAA3's 'every ordain-path CB job now starts with preflight' lift silently aligned the four entry points to that pattern, and only the conjure parser was already at [1] — the enshrine parser stayed at [0] and was guaranteed to fail on first post-BBAA3 empirical enshrine. Same 'first empirical run after BBAA3 preflight landing' bug class previously surfaced as 9ea3b9fa (CB automapSubstitutions rejecting bash default-value syntax in preflight script), 33798bfc (alpine missing /bin/bash), 8c057a45 (3-curl-fail orchestrator-kill that motivated the curl primitive); same dca68895 deferral-to-BBAAs precedent. Edit shape: line 1040 buildStepOutputs[0]→buildStepOutputs[1]; line 1036 comment expanded from 'buildStepOutputs[0] is base64-encoded JSON from the enshrine step' to 'buildStepOutputs[1] is base64-encoded JSON from the enshrine-copy step; step 0 is the in-pool reliquary preflight, which does not write /builder/outputs/output' — load-bearing per CLAUDE.md (without the WHY a future reader sees an arbitrary-looking [1] and could 'fix' back to [0]). Operator chose minimal over robust: the more defensive variant `[.results.buildStepOutputs[]? | select(. != "")] | first // empty` was discussed and deferred — would decouple parser from preflight-step positioning at the cost of breaking the implicit 'step 1 is enshrine-copy' positional contract; minimal preserves symmetry with the line 1264 conjure parser (also positional [1]) and bounds future-shuffling cost to a one-line review of two paired sites. Sweep follow-up not done this notch and worth tracking: grep buildStepOutputs[0-9]+ across the codebase to confirm bind/graft/about/vouch parsers don't have parallel off-by-one fingerprints (dca68895's docket gap explicitly flagged that standalone vouch and standalone about are reached by reliquary-preflight via conjure/bind/graft flow but were not enumerated in the four direct entry points). Verification: bash -n green on rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh; tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh 25/26 with rbtdrf_rv_rbrv_all_vessels pre-existing fail unchanged from BBAA3/BBAA4/BBAAs baseline — no new failures from this notch; empirical re-run of onboarding-sequence pending operator decision since per docket iteration loop the only sanctioned action after fix-recommend is await guidance, which was given as 'apply minimal fix and notch'.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                
8c057a45  ₢BBAAs    BCG-conformant build-poll diagnostics + extract rbgu_http_request primitive. Empirical onboarding-sequence run after the alpine-shebang fix surfaced a new failure mode in conjure-srjcl: 3 consecutive curl failures at poll 131 killed the orchestrator while the underlying Cloud Build was healthy and still running (build d033f203 SUCCESS at 17:26:23Z; all 5 SLSA artifacts landed in GAR). Forensic gap surfaced: bare '-s' on the poll-loop curl swallowed stderr entirely, '${z_curl_rc}' was captured into a local but never logged anywhere, and four loop-internal temp files (ZRBFC_BUILD_STATUS_FILE, ZRBFC_STATUS_CHECK_FILE, the literal rbfc_poll_err_check.txt, and the missing curl-stderr file) were single-named-overwriting on every attempt — violating BCG §'In loops, use an auto-incrementing integer for uniqueness' (BCG line 536-553). Survey of curl callsites found three convergent patterns (rbgu_http_json, zrbgv_http_get_with_5xx_retry, rbgo_exchange_jwt_capture) that all share the BCG-conformant inline shape: '-sS', stderr-to-temp-file, surface rc + stderr-file in warn, but each module reimplements the curl flag boilerplate inline. Discussed extracting a primitive helper now vs continuing to inline — went with helper extraction (Option C) since rbfc would otherwise be the 4th inline site, and a follow-up pace will port rbgu_http_json+zrbgv_http_get_with_5xx_retry to the helper for DRY treatment. Helper rbgu_http_request lives in rbgu_Utility.sh adjacent to rbgu_http_json (rbgu kindle is already a dependency of rbfd_cli before zrbfc_kindle, verified). Signature: method/url/token/resp_file/code_file/stderr_file/[body_file] — method-parameterized so future port is mechanical; hardcoded Authorization/Accept headers and conditional Content-Type for body cases (no array param needed since the three convergent callsites have header sets derivable from {method, token, has-body}; bash 3.2 array-as-parameter awkwardness avoided per BCG). Returns curl exit rc — caller decides policy. Refactored zrbfc_wait_build_completion: per-poll-attempt files numbered by z_polls (already a loop counter, no new variable per BCG Exception 2); failure-branch warnings now surface 'rc=${z_curl_rc}' and 'see ${z_stderr_file}' (mirrors rbgv:140 wording exactly); added buc_log_pipe of stderr file content into transcript; tracks z_last_good_response and copies to ZRBFC_BUILD_STATUS_FILE at loop exit (rbgu's 'register winner under bare infix' pattern at rbgu:357-359). External consumers of ZRBFC_BUILD_STATUS_FILE in rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh:1040,1264 unaffected — bare-name contract preserved. Removed unused ZRBFC_STATUS_CHECK_FILE kindle constant (no external consumers). Tolerance at z_max_consecutive_failures=3 unchanged — Pass-1 collects rc data; if next empirical failure surfaces a real >60s outage, Pass-2 calibrates tolerance on observed rc distribution rather than guessing. Behavior delta on rbfc poll: now sends 'Accept: application/json' header it previously omitted (Cloud Build REST API returns JSON regardless of Accept; benign canonicalization). Verification: bash -n green on both files; tt/rbtd-b.Build.sh green; tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh 25/26 (rbtdrf_rv_rbrv_all_vessels pre-existing fail unchanged from BBAA3/BBAA4 baseline); shellcheck warnings on both files are all pre-existing (kindle vars consumed cross-module, $(<file) on local declarations) — no new warnings from this notch. 33798bfc  ₢BBAAs    fix in-pool reliquary preflight shebang for alpine: switch from #!/bin/bash to #!/bin/sh in both the source script (rbgjr01-reliquary-preflight.sh:1) and the preflight step assembly (zrbfc_assemble_preflight_step in rbfc_FoundryCore.sh:551, which strips line 1 of the source and re-prepends a shebang). Empirical onboarding-sequence run after the substitution-syntax fix surfaced a new Cloud Build failure: step 0 'reliquary-preflight' on the alpine reliquary image exited 255 with 'exec script: no such file or directory' — the classic missing-interpreter ENOENT, here for /bin/bash since alpine ships busybox sh only and bash is not installed. Convention check across the 14 step scripts in rbgj* tree (rbgja*, rbgjb*, rbgje*, rbgji*, rbgjm*, rbgjr*, rbgjv*) showed an established precedent: 13 of 14 scripts use #!/bin/bash and run on docker/gcloud images that ship bash; the one outlier rbgjv01-download-verifier.sh uses #!/bin/sh and runs on the alpine reliquary tool image — wired via the explicit shell column 'sh' in the rbfc_FoundryCore.sh:469 step-defs table. The new preflight broke the pattern by advertising bash while running on alpine. Edit #2 (the assembly's printf) is the load-bearing one — the assembly's tail -n +2 strips the source shebang anyway, so the source line never reaches Cloud Build. Edit #1 (the source shebang) keeps the file consistent with the rbgjv01 precedent that source shebang matches runtime shell. Script body is already POSIX-compatible: test, case, for, ${VAR%:*}, ${VAR##*:}, printf, grep/cut, [ -z ], >&2; the only mild non-POSIX is set -euo pipefail which busybox ash has supported since 1.27 (2017) and modern alpine ships >=1.36. Verification: bash -n green on both edited files; full re-run pending after operator restarts the chat in clean pace remount. 9ea3b9fa  ₢BBAAs    fix in-pool reliquary preflight script: drop bash default-value syntax from the three enshrine-locator reads so Cloud Build's automapSubstitutions scanner recognizes them as references. Empirical onboarding-sequence run surfaced HTTP 400 'key _RBGR_ENSHRINE_LOCATOR_n in the substitution data is not matched in the template' on the very first ordain (conjure of rbev-sentry-deb-tether). Direct inspection of the rejected build payload (rbfd_context_build.json) showed the four GAR/RELIQUARY substitutions referenced as ${_KEY} bare in the script body — accepted by Cloud Build — and the three ENSHRINE_LOCATOR_n substitutions referenced as ${_KEY:-} (bash default-value form) — rejected. Only structural difference between matched and unmatched. The runtime guard one line below (test -n LOCATOR || continue) already handles the empty-string case for non-conjure modes, so the :- defaults were doing no semantic work. This was the BBAA3 wrap-criterion run that dca68895 explicitly deferred to BBAAs; the bug was guaranteed to surface on first empirical submission. content.content:
1	# jjezs_paddock ₣BB
2	
3	## Context
4	
5	Release qualification gap surfaced by the ₣A_ depot-regen heat. Every existing test tier (`rbw-tf` fast, `rbw-tr` release) tolerates accumulated depot state, so silent first-build assumptions slip through. Recent spooks — kludge-aware-charge-prereq (k-prefixed hallmarks at GAR), ZRBOB_PROJECT (compose project name without runtime prefix), rbob_charged_predicate prefix omission, reliquary integrity-broken on negative test — all share a property: they only manifest on first-build paths, and none were caught until live verification surfaced them.
6	
7	This heat constructs `rbw-tP` QualifyPristine — a third tier that **refuses to run** unless marshal-zero state was just committed. The refusal is enforced by the test itself, not by ceremony or operator discipline.
8	
9	## Single operator prerequisite chain
10	
11	Operator has exactly one prerequisite chain: confirm Payor health → marshal-zero → commit → run `rbw-tP`. Payor OAuth is the only credential the operator must have ready; everything else (governor, retriever, director SAs and their RBRA files) is **minted by the qualification itself**, not restored from backup.
12	
13	## Entry contract — load-bearing
14	
15	`rbw-tP` fails-fast unless ALL of the following hold:
16	
17	- Working tree clean
18	- HEAD commit is a marshal-zero commit (detectable signature baked into `rblm_zero` per BBAAB)
19	- RBRR fields are blank (prefixes, depot project ID, etc.)
20	- No RBRA credential files present (governor, retriever, director — all deleted by marshal-zero, recreated by the qualification)
21	- No hallmark fields populated in nameplates
22	- No depot-scoped fields populated in vessel rbrv.env files
23	
24	Operator cannot skip the prerequisite. This is the property that makes the tier catch the silent-first-build bug class by construction.
25	
26	## Failure mode contract
27	
28	Mid-qualification failure means start-over-from-zero, not patch-and-continue. Documented explicitly in runbook to prevent the very accumulated-state bug class this tier exists to catch.
29	
30	## Release-branch execution contract
31	
32	Three properties bind every fixture and phase in this tier.
33	
34	**Run on a release branch.** The release machinery is engineered for branch execution, not main. The branch will accumulate many commits across a successful pristine run. After a failed run, the branch holds bygone commits that `rbw-MZ` does not touch — they remain as history; the next run starts from a fresh marshal-zero commit on top.
35	
36	**Commits during the run are first-class.** Each step that mutates regime state (rbrr.env, rbra files, vessel rbrv.env, etc.) commits the change. Container-recipe work has a documented cognitive failure mode: humans lose track of where they are in long sequences. The growing commit trail is the operator's mental anchor against that.
37	
38	**Stop on very first failure, cleanly and clearly.** A step fails → fixture stops → operator stops → debugging happens immediately on the failed branch. Stderr names the failed step; fixture exits non-zero. Recovery is `rbw-MZ` + retry on a fresh marshal-zero. No graceful degradation, no recovery branches.
39	
40	Engineering scaffolding for any other shape — partial-run cleanup, soft-delete tolerance, multi-mode failure handling, recovery-time orphan inspection, diagnostic-redundancy — is non-load-bearing and does not belong in pristine-tier code. The single mechanical path is the entire surface.
41	
42	## Tier layering
43	
44	Three tiers, escalating cost:
45	
46	| Colophon | Frontispiece | Cost | Entry contract |
47	|----------|--------------|------|----------------|
48	| `rbw-tf` | QualifyFast | seconds | none |
49	| `rbw-tr` | QualifyRelease | minutes | none (accumulated state OK) |
50	| `rbw-tP` | QualifyPristine *(new)* | ~1 hour + cloud $ | marshal-zero just committed |
51	
52	`rbw-tP` is THE release gate. `rbw-tr` becomes the pre-pristine smoke test — cheap-and-frequent for development confidence; pristine for actual release.
53	
54	## Gauntlet test suite — design
55	
56	`rbw-tP` exec's the **gauntlet suite** — a TestSuite (not a custom orchestrator script) composed of fixtures sequenced from marshal-zero state through canonical-credentialed state to crucible verification. The TestSuite mechanism is existing infrastructure (`tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.{fast,service,crucible,complete}.sh`); the gauntlet suite extends it.
57	
58	**Suite-of-fixtures, not orchestrator-script.** The original construction plan (custom bash orchestrator with inline tabtarget calls) was abandoned in favor of suite composition. Each section of the release-qualification sequence becomes a fixture; the suite composes them in order.
59	
60	**Naming hygiene.** "Gauntlet" is the suite name; "pristine" is reserved for the §1 pristine-lifecycle fixture and informally for tier vocabulary. The suite name does not appear at fixture or case level — distinct stratification across naming layers (suite ≠ fixture ≠ case).
61	
62	**State ladder, fixtures along it.** The suite walks a state ladder no single fixture covers:
63	
64	1. enrollment-validation (preflight, state-indifferent)
65	2. pristine-lifecycle (§1: marshal-zero gate + throwaway depot/SA lifecycle)
66	3. canonical-establish (§2: canonical depot levy + governor mantle + retriever invest + director invest + IAM propagation wait)
67	4. canonical-onboarding-sequence (§3: reliquary inscribe + per-vessel hallmark ordain — handbook-driven build walks per BBAAf)
68	5. regime-validation, regime-smoke (post-§3, regimes now populated)
69	6. four-mode (cloud-build mode coverage)
70	7. tadmor, moriah, srjcl, pluml (§4: per-crucible test fixtures, vessel-exists preconditions per BBAAs)
71	
72	No automatic teardown — see "No-teardown decision" below.
73	
74	## Dual purpose: handbook-regression coverage
75	
76	The gauntlet's primary purpose is release qualification (catch silent first-build assumptions). Its **secondary purpose** is automated handbook-regression coverage: ensure operator-facing onboarding handbook tracks (`rbh*`) stay in sync with working code so future operators following the prescribed steps aren't surprised by drift.
77	
78	Build-side handbook coverage lives in BBAAf (canonical-onboarding-sequence): each ordain case walks the corresponding director-mode handbook track (`rbw-Odf` conjure / `rbw-Oda` airgap / `rbw-Odb` bind / `rbw-Odg` graft) rather than calling inscribe/ordain machinery directly.
79	
80	**Build/run split — load-bearing.** Handbook walks cover the BUILD step (vessel construction) and stop when the hallmark lands in GAR. Run-side fixtures (per-crucible: tadmor/moriah/srjcl/pluml) stay as direct test fixtures with vessel-exists preconditions, NOT handbook walks. The split keeps run-side reusable against any vessel source — coupling them to handbook prose breaks reusability.
81	
82	Drift between fixtures and handbooks is accepted future maintenance: whoever changes one is responsible for updating the other; the gauntlet's job is to surface drift, not prevent it by construction.
83	
84	Run-side handbook coverage (`rbw-Ots`, the rack portion of `rbw-Ofc`) is explicitly out of scope for this heat.
85	
86	## Fixture disposition — Independent vs StateProgressing
87	
88	Each fixture declares a disposition flag in Rust, controlling engine behavior:
89	
90	- **Independent** — cases are self-contained; suite-order is informational. Engine permits keep-going mode for surveying.
91	- **StateProgressing** — case N's success establishes preconditions for case N+1. Engine refuses keep-going mode (incoherent: failed case leaves broken precondition). Fail-fast required.
92	
93	**Default is Independent.** Existing fixtures (`enrollment-validation`, `regime-validation`, `regime-smoke`, `four-mode`, `tadmor`, `moriah`, `srjcl`, `pluml`) are Independent and require no tagging change. Only fixtures whose case-N success establishes case-N+1 preconditions get StateProgressing — currently `pristine-lifecycle`, `canonical-establish`, and `canonical-onboarding-sequence`.
94	
95	Suite-level disposition derives from membership: any StateProgressing fixture in the suite makes the suite state-progressing → suite-level keep-going refused.
96	
97	**Per-case precondition probes.** Each case in a StateProgressing fixture probes its precondition at start. If state matches expectation, run. If not, refuse with operator-actionable diagnostic naming the expected state and the closest fixture that produces it. This enables safe a-la-carte single-case rerun — the engine doesn't need to know the case's history; the case's probe enforces.
98	
99	## Suite-level fail-fast
100	
101	Across all suites — not just gauntlet — any fixture failure stops subsequent fixtures from running automatically. State-progressing suites require this for correctness; independent suites adopt it for the simpler mental model. Operators who want full surveying run individual fixtures separately.
102	
103	## No-teardown decision
104	
105	The gauntlet suite omits an automatic teardown phase. After green tally, the canonical depot, SAs, hallmarks, and RBRA files persist for operator inspection. Deliberate trade-off:
106	
107	- **Inspect-after-success** is genuine operator value — browse canonical depot in GCP console, inspect GAR contents, run ad-hoc verification a test sequence cannot anticipate.
108	- **Cleanup is operator-driven** — manual sequence is `rbw-fA` per vessel + `rbw-arD` + `rbw-adD` + `rbw-dU` (governor cleanup folds into unmake per BBAAN). All existing tabtargets; no new infrastructure.
109	- **Failed runs don't accumulate cleanup debt** — under fail-fast, teardown wouldn't run anyway; the no-teardown decision only changes behavior on full success.
110	- **Cost visibility** — pending-delete soft-deleted projects accumulate per run (RELEASE.md accepts this); not changed by teardown decision.
111	
112	Documentation of the post-success cleanup ceremony is BBAAI work (RELEASE.md).
113	
114	## Operator scope
115	
116	Single operator (project lead). Not designed for multi-operator workflow. The README.md release section remains the runbook for the gauntlet itself; the dual-purpose handbook-regression coverage protects the broader operator surface (onboarding tracks under `rbh*`) from drift.
117	
118	## Coupling to ₣A_
119	
120	Cutover work from ₣A_ informing `rbw-tP`'s sequence has landed (BBAAM depot-identity-collapse). ₣A_'s remaining paces run independently of BB.
121	
122	## Locked design decisions
123	
124	- **Depot identity collapse**: RBRR collapses to `RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER`; `RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX` flows through depot-affiliated resources; per-moniker depot fact-files; pristine-fixture moniker autodetect lives in Rust (`rbtdrp_pristine.rs`), not payor.
125	- **Cult-verb naming**: SA domain muster→**roster**; image domain muster→**audit**. Domain-exclusive split; lowercase tail letters preserved for no-cloud-change observation colophons.
126	- **Build/run split**: handbook walks cover build only; per-crucible fixtures stay reusable as direct tests.
127	
128	## Calibrant fixture family — BBAAh
129	
130	Synthetic Rust fixtures with deterministic verdicts, registered in the manifest by BBAAh. The family exists to give ₣BC `rbk-mvp-3-theurge-self-test` deterministic targets for bash-blackbox driving and manifest-coverage validation; here in ₣BB only the foundation lands.
131	
132	**Anchor word: `calibrant`.** Module `rbtdrl_calibrant.rs` (classifier `l` for caLibrant since `c` is owned by crucible). Replaces the dormant `rbtdrd_dummy.rs` placeholder. Test counterpart `rbtdtl_calibrant.rs`. Internal-only vocabulary; not end-user-facing.
133	
134	**Fixture catalog** (Rust, manifest-registered):
135	
136	- `calibrant-verdicts` (Independent) — pass / fail / skip / pass_with_output. Verdict-path coverage. The case-written file (`output.txt`) is distinct from the engine-auto `trace.txt`.
137	- `calibrant-fail-fast` (Independent, multi-section) — fixture-internal fail-fast across cases and across sections; "not_reached" cases write sentinel files used as absence-of-side-effect assertions.
138	- `calibrant-progressing` (StateProgressing) — exercises `rbtdrb_Probe` diagnostic format and `rbtdre_resolve_fail_fast` policy gate.
139	- `calibrant-sentinel` (Independent) — single case writing a known file when it runs. Used for suite-level fail-fast verification.
140	
141	Suite: `calibrant` = [verdicts, fail-fast, sentinel]. Sentinel-after-fail verifies cross-fixture fail-fast contract.
142	
143	Downstream consumption — bash testbench, manifest-coverage runtime checks, coverage-validation fixtures and driver — lives in ₣BC.
144	
145	## References
146	
147	- ₣A_ rbk-mvp-3-resource-prefix-and-depot-regen — surfaced the gap
148	- ₣BC rbk-mvp-3-theurge-self-test — calibrant downstream consumers
149	- `tt/rbw-tr.QualifyRelease.sh` — current release qualify; layered alongside, not replaced
150	- `Tools/rbk/rblm_cli.sh` — `rbw-MZ` zeroes local regime; marshal-zero signature baked here per BBAAB
151	- `.claude/commands/rbk-prep-release.md` — upstream contribution ceremony; pristine-pass becomes a precondition (BBAAH)
152	
153	# jjezs_pace ₢BBAAs
154	
155	## Character
156	
157	Empirical run-until-green for `onboarding-sequence` against a canonical-established depot.
158	
159	## Iteration loop
160	
161	Each turn:
162	
163	1. Run the fixture, or the named failing case.
164	2. If needed, use `gcloud` to diagnose.
165	3. Recommend a fix.
166	4. Await guidance.
167	
168	No autonomous re-runs. No autonomous edits.
169	
170	## In-flight progress
171	
172	Empirical run on 2026-04-29 (post `c547c62a`) was stopped mid-fixture by operator (had to leave). Cases that passed before stop:
173	
174	1. `inscribe-reliquary` PASSED
175	2. `kludge-tadmor` PASSED — first green confirmation of the kludge/ordain self-veto fixes (`142cf920` + `c547c62a`).
176	3. `kludge-ccyolo` PASSED.
177	
178	Cases not yet observed in this run: `conjure-srjcl`, `ordain-airgap`, `propagate-moriah`, `ordain-pluml`, `ordain-graft`. Next mount: re-run from the top against still-valid canonical depot+SAs (case probes will gate); if precondition state still holds, advance through the remaining cases and diagnose the first new failure.
179	
180	## Wrap criterion
181	
182	Green tally on onboarding-sequence.
183	
184	## Cross-pace dependencies

187	- canonical-establish (BBAAe) for depot+SAs.

# jjezs_paddock ₣BB

## Context

Release qualification gap surfaced by the ₣A_ depot-regen heat. Every existing test tier (`rbw-tf` fast, `rbw-tr` release) tolerates accumulated depot state, so silent first-build assumptions slip through. Recent spooks — kludge-aware-charge-prereq (k-prefixed hallmarks at GAR), ZRBOB_PROJECT (compose project name without runtime prefix), rbob_charged_predicate prefix omission, reliquary integrity-broken on negative test — all share a property: they only manifest on first-build paths, and none were caught until live verification surfaced them.

This heat constructs `rbw-tP` QualifyPristine — a third tier that **refuses to run** unless marshal-zero state was just committed. The refusal is enforced by the test itself, not by ceremony or operator discipline.

## Single operator prerequisite chain

Operator has exactly one prerequisite chain: confirm Payor health → marshal-zero → commit → run `rbw-tP`. Payor OAuth is the only credential the operator must have ready; everything else (governor, retriever, director SAs and their RBRA files) is **minted by the qualification itself**, not restored from backup.

## Entry contract — load-bearing

`rbw-tP` fails-fast unless ALL of the following hold:

- Working tree clean
- HEAD commit is a marshal-zero commit (detectable signature baked into `rblm_zero` per BBAAB)
- RBRR fields are blank (prefixes, depot project ID, etc.)
- No RBRA credential files present (governor, retriever, director — all deleted by marshal-zero, recreated by the qualification)
- No hallmark fields populated in nameplates
- No depot-scoped fields populated in vessel rbrv.env files

Operator cannot skip the prerequisite. This is the property that makes the tier catch the silent-first-build bug class by construction.

## Failure mode contract

Mid-qualification failure means start-over-from-zero, not patch-and-continue. Documented explicitly in runbook to prevent the very accumulated-state bug class this tier exists to catch.

## Release-branch execution contract

Three properties bind every fixture and phase in this tier.

**Run on a release branch.** The release machinery is engineered for branch execution, not main. The branch will accumulate many commits across a successful pristine run. After a failed run, the branch holds bygone commits that `rbw-MZ` does not touch — they remain as history; the next run starts from a fresh marshal-zero commit on top.

**Commits during the run are first-class.** Each step that mutates regime state (rbrr.env, rbra files, vessel rbrv.env, etc.) commits the change. Container-recipe work has a documented cognitive failure mode: humans lose track of where they are in long sequences. The growing commit trail is the operator's mental anchor against that.

**Stop on very first failure, cleanly and clearly.** A step fails → fixture stops → operator stops → debugging happens immediately on the failed branch. Stderr names the failed step; fixture exits non-zero. Recovery is `rbw-MZ` + retry on a fresh marshal-zero. No graceful degradation, no recovery branches.

Engineering scaffolding for any other shape — partial-run cleanup, soft-delete tolerance, multi-mode failure handling, recovery-time orphan inspection, diagnostic-redundancy — is non-load-bearing and does not belong in pristine-tier code. The single mechanical path is the entire surface.

## Tier layering

Three tiers, escalating cost:

| Colophon | Frontispiece | Cost | Entry contract |
|----------|--------------|------|----------------|
| `rbw-tf` | QualifyFast | seconds | none |
| `rbw-tr` | QualifyRelease | minutes | none (accumulated state OK) |
| `rbw-tP` | QualifyPristine *(new)* | ~1 hour + cloud $ | marshal-zero just committed |

`rbw-tP` is THE release gate. `rbw-tr` becomes the pre-pristine smoke test — cheap-and-frequent for development confidence; pristine for actual release.

## Gauntlet test suite — design

`rbw-tP` exec's the **gauntlet suite** — a TestSuite (not a custom orchestrator script) composed of fixtures sequenced from marshal-zero state through canonical-credentialed state to crucible verification. The TestSuite mechanism is existing infrastructure (`tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.{fast,service,crucible,complete}.sh`); the gauntlet suite extends it.

**Suite-of-fixtures, not orchestrator-script.** The original construction plan (custom bash orchestrator with inline tabtarget calls) was abandoned in favor of suite composition. Each section of the release-qualification sequence becomes a fixture; the suite composes them in order.

**Naming hygiene.** "Gauntlet" is the suite name; "pristine" is reserved for the §1 pristine-lifecycle fixture and informally for tier vocabulary. The suite name does not appear at fixture or case level — distinct stratification across naming layers (suite ≠ fixture ≠ case).

**State ladder, fixtures along it.** The suite walks a state ladder no single fixture covers:

1. enrollment-validation (preflight, state-indifferent)
2. pristine-lifecycle (§1: marshal-zero gate + throwaway depot/SA lifecycle)
3. canonical-establish (§2: canonical depot levy + governor mantle + retriever invest + director invest + IAM propagation wait)
4. canonical-onboarding-sequence (§3: reliquary inscribe + per-vessel hallmark ordain — handbook-driven build walks per BBAAf)
5. regime-validation, regime-smoke (post-§3, regimes now populated)
6. four-mode (cloud-build mode coverage)
7. tadmor, moriah, srjcl, pluml (§4: per-crucible test fixtures, vessel-exists preconditions per BBAAs)

No automatic teardown — see "No-teardown decision" below.

## Dual purpose: handbook-regression coverage

The gauntlet's primary purpose is release qualification (catch silent first-build assumptions). Its **secondary purpose** is automated handbook-regression coverage: ensure operator-facing onboarding handbook tracks (`rbh*`) stay in sync with working code so future operators following the prescribed steps aren't surprised by drift.

Build-side handbook coverage lives in BBAAf (canonical-onboarding-sequence): each ordain case walks the corresponding director-mode handbook track (`rbw-Odf` conjure / `rbw-Oda` airgap / `rbw-Odb` bind / `rbw-Odg` graft) rather than calling inscribe/ordain machinery directly.

**Build/run split — load-bearing.** Handbook walks cover the BUILD step (vessel construction) and stop when the hallmark lands in GAR. Run-side fixtures (per-crucible: tadmor/moriah/srjcl/pluml) stay as direct test fixtures with vessel-exists preconditions, NOT handbook walks. The split keeps run-side reusable against any vessel source — coupling them to handbook prose breaks reusability.

Drift between fixtures and handbooks is accepted future maintenance: whoever changes one is responsible for updating the other; the gauntlet's job is to surface drift, not prevent it by construction.

Run-side handbook coverage (`rbw-Ots`, the rack portion of `rbw-Ofc`) is explicitly out of scope for this heat.

## Fixture disposition — Independent vs StateProgressing

Each fixture declares a disposition flag in Rust, controlling engine behavior:

- **Independent** — cases are self-contained; suite-order is informational. Engine permits keep-going mode for surveying.
- **StateProgressing** — case N's success establishes preconditions for case N+1. Engine refuses keep-going mode (incoherent: failed case leaves broken precondition). Fail-fast required.

**Default is Independent.** Existing fixtures (`enrollment-validation`, `regime-validation`, `regime-smoke`, `four-mode`, `tadmor`, `moriah`, `srjcl`, `pluml`) are Independent and require no tagging change. Only fixtures whose case-N success establishes case-N+1 preconditions get StateProgressing — currently `pristine-lifecycle`, `canonical-establish`, and `canonical-onboarding-sequence`.

Suite-level disposition derives from membership: any StateProgressing fixture in the suite makes the suite state-progressing → suite-level keep-going refused.

**Per-case precondition probes.** Each case in a StateProgressing fixture probes its precondition at start. If state matches expectation, run. If not, refuse with operator-actionable diagnostic naming the expected state and the closest fixture that produces it. This enables safe a-la-carte single-case rerun — the engine doesn't need to know the case's history; the case's probe enforces.

## Suite-level fail-fast

Across all suites — not just gauntlet — any fixture failure stops subsequent fixtures from running automatically. State-progressing suites require this for correctness; independent suites adopt it for the simpler mental model. Operators who want full surveying run individual fixtures separately.

## No-teardown decision

The gauntlet suite omits an automatic teardown phase. After green tally, the canonical depot, SAs, hallmarks, and RBRA files persist for operator inspection. Deliberate trade-off:

- **Inspect-after-success** is genuine operator value — browse canonical depot in GCP console, inspect GAR contents, run ad-hoc verification a test sequence cannot anticipate.
- **Cleanup is operator-driven** — manual sequence is `rbw-fA` per vessel + `rbw-arD` + `rbw-adD` + `rbw-dU` (governor cleanup folds into unmake per BBAAN). All existing tabtargets; no new infrastructure.
- **Failed runs don't accumulate cleanup debt** — under fail-fast, teardown wouldn't run anyway; the no-teardown decision only changes behavior on full success.
- **Cost visibility** — pending-delete soft-deleted projects accumulate per run (RELEASE.md accepts this); not changed by teardown decision.

Documentation of the post-success cleanup ceremony is BBAAI work (RELEASE.md).

## Operator scope

Single operator (project lead). Not designed for multi-operator workflow. The README.md release section remains the runbook for the gauntlet itself; the dual-purpose handbook-regression coverage protects the broader operator surface (onboarding tracks under `rbh*`) from drift.

## Coupling to ₣A_

Cutover work from ₣A_ informing `rbw-tP`'s sequence has landed (BBAAM depot-identity-collapse). ₣A_'s remaining paces run independently of BB.

## Locked design decisions

- **Depot identity collapse**: RBRR collapses to `RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER`; `RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX` flows through depot-affiliated resources; per-moniker depot fact-files; pristine-fixture moniker autodetect lives in Rust (`rbtdrp_pristine.rs`), not payor.
- **Cult-verb naming**: SA domain muster→**roster**; image domain muster→**audit**. Domain-exclusive split; lowercase tail letters preserved for no-cloud-change observation colophons.
- **Build/run split**: handbook walks cover build only; per-crucible fixtures stay reusable as direct tests.

## Calibrant fixture family — BBAAh

Synthetic Rust fixtures with deterministic verdicts, registered in the manifest by BBAAh. The family exists to give ₣BC `rbk-mvp-3-theurge-self-test` deterministic targets for bash-blackbox driving and manifest-coverage validation; here in ₣BB only the foundation lands.

**Anchor word: `calibrant`.** Module `rbtdrl_calibrant.rs` (classifier `l` for caLibrant since `c` is owned by crucible). Replaces the dormant `rbtdrd_dummy.rs` placeholder. Test counterpart `rbtdtl_calibrant.rs`. Internal-only vocabulary; not end-user-facing.

**Fixture catalog** (Rust, manifest-registered):

- `calibrant-verdicts` (Independent) — pass / fail / skip / pass_with_output. Verdict-path coverage. The case-written file (`output.txt`) is distinct from the engine-auto `trace.txt`.
- `calibrant-fail-fast` (Independent, multi-section) — fixture-internal fail-fast across cases and across sections; "not_reached" cases write sentinel files used as absence-of-side-effect assertions.
- `calibrant-progressing` (StateProgressing) — exercises `rbtdrb_Probe` diagnostic format and `rbtdre_resolve_fail_fast` policy gate.
- `calibrant-sentinel` (Independent) — single case writing a known file when it runs. Used for suite-level fail-fast verification.

Suite: `calibrant` = [verdicts, fail-fast, sentinel]. Sentinel-after-fail verifies cross-fixture fail-fast contract.

Downstream consumption — bash testbench, manifest-coverage runtime checks, coverage-validation fixtures and driver — lives in ₣BC.

## References

- ₣A_ rbk-mvp-3-resource-prefix-and-depot-regen — surfaced the gap
- ₣BC rbk-mvp-3-theurge-self-test — calibrant downstream consumers
- `tt/rbw-tr.QualifyRelease.sh` — current release qualify; layered alongside, not replaced
- `Tools/rbk/rblm_cli.sh` — `rbw-MZ` zeroes local regime; marshal-zero signature baked here per BBAAB
- `.claude/commands/rbk-prep-release.md` — upstream contribution ceremony; pristine-pass becomes a precondition (BBAAH)

# jjezs_pace ₢BBAAs

## Character

Empirical run-until-green for `onboarding-sequence` against a canonical-established depot.

## Iteration loop

Each turn:

1. Run the fixture, or the named failing case.
2. If needed, use `gcloud` to diagnose.
3. Recommend a fix.
4. Await guidance.

No autonomous re-runs. No autonomous edits.

## In-flight progress

Empirical run on 2026-04-29 (post `c547c62a`) was stopped mid-fixture by operator (had to leave). Cases that passed before stop:

1. `inscribe-reliquary` PASSED
2. `kludge-tadmor` PASSED — first green confirmation of the kludge/ordain self-veto fixes (`142cf920` + `c547c62a`).
3. `kludge-ccyolo` PASSED.

Cases not yet observed in this run: `conjure-srjcl`, `ordain-airgap`, `propagate-moriah`, `ordain-pluml`, `ordain-graft`. Next mount: re-run from the top against still-valid canonical depot+SAs (case probes will gate); if precondition state still holds, advance through the remaining cases and diagnose the first new failure.

## Wrap criterion

Green tally on onboarding-sequence.

## Cross-pace dependencies

- BBAAu landed.
- canonical-establish (BBAAe) for depot+SAs.

## Out of scope

- Construction — BBAAu.
- Full gauntlet — BBAAX.
- moriah Fixture static — phantom.

Empirical post-BBAAs onboarding-sequence run (5/8 PASSED) hit `rbfd_enshrine: Vessel argument required (sigil or path)` on ordain-airgap's enshrine of rbev-bottle-ifrit-forge, with the diagnostic `WARNING: Colophon 'rbw-dE' takes no folio; ignoring unexpected argument(s): rbev-bottle-ifrit-forge` confirming the dispatcher stripped the arg before rbfd_enshrine's `${1:-}` could read it. Root cause: concurrent commit 17b3f85e (₢A-AAj, landed 2026-05-02 12:28:38 mid-fixture-run) tightened buz_exec_lookup's empty-channel branch from silent-pass-through to warn-and-clear ("Friendly-error UX extension: empty-channel commands now warn-and-clear when given unexpected positional args instead of silently passing them through to the formulary") to surface bogus args to truly-no-folio commands like buw-rnl. The change was correct in intent but unsynchronized: many rbw-* enrollments declared channel `""` while their handlers in rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh, rbfl_FoundryLedger.sh, rbfr_FoundryRetriever.sh, and rbfc_FoundryCore.sh read `${1:-}` for a folio they had been receiving via the prior silent-pass-through. ordain-conjure (case 4) passed because it ran at 12:03:45 — pre-17b3f85e — under the old behavior; enshrine-upstream ran at 12:30:13 — post-17b3f85e — under the new behavior and died on empty `${1:-}`. Fix shape mirrors the prior yoke fix (commit 4097610f, BBAAs): change channel `""` to `"param1"` in zipper enrollment, change handler's folio read from `${1:-}` to `${BUZ_FOLIO:-}`, and (for handlers with positional --force flag) shift the flag read from `${2:-}` to `${1:-}` since the dispatcher now consumes the folio and forwards remaining args. Affected colophons (orchestrator-passes-args set, identified via grep of rbtdri_invoke_global call sites in Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrc_crucible.rs and rbtdro_onboarding.rs): rbw-dE (rbfd_enshrine, vessel), rbw-fO (rbfd_ordain, vessel), rbw-fk (rbfd_kludge, vessel), rbw-fA (rbfl_abjure, hallmark + --force), rbw-fs (rbfr_summon, hallmark), rbw-fpf/rbw-fpc (rbfc_plumb_full/compact, hallmark), rbw-irh/rbw-irr (rbfl_rekon_hallmark/reliquary, hallmark/stamp), rbw-iwh/rbw-iwr/rbw-iwe (rbfl_wrest, locator), rbw-iJh/rbw-iJr/rbw-iJe (rbfl_jettison, locator + --force) — 14 enrollments, 11 distinct handlers (3 wrest + 3 jettison enrollments share their handlers). Excluded from sweep (genuinely no-folio, channel `""` correct): rbw-aM mantle, rbw-arr/rbw-adr roster, rbw-dL/dU/dl depot ops, rbw-dI inscribe, rbw-gPI/PE/PR/q payor, rbw-Occ/Ocr/Ocd/Ofc/Ots/Odf/Oda/Odb/Odg/Op/Og/o onboarding handbooks, rbw-iah/iar/iae audit, rbw-MZ/MP/MG marshal, rbw-ft tally, rbw-fV vouch — these handlers take no folio and the new warn-and-clear behavior catches operator typos correctly. BCG note on furnish: unlike the rbfl_yoke fix which moved zrbrv_kindle to rbfl_cli.sh's furnish per BCG "kindle is CLI-only" rule (yoke needed to write to a kindle constant RBRV_RELIQUARY before validation), the handlers in this sweep call `zrbfc_resolve_vessel`/`zrbfc_load_vessel` (pure source, no kindle) or consume hallmark/locator/stamp strings (no regime state at all), so reading BUZ_FOLIO from the function body is BCG-conformant and no CLI furnish changes are needed in rbfd_cli.sh, rbfl_cli.sh, rbfr_cli.sh, or rbfc_cli.sh. The dispatcher's `BUZ_FOLIO="${z_folio}" exec ...` (buz_zipper.sh:280) makes the folio available as an inherited environment variable to the function body. Tabtarget context regenerated via tt/rbw-MG.MarshalGenerate.sh — the Folio column for the 14 affected rows flips from `—` (empty channel) to `param1`, satisfying the qualify-fast freshness gate. Verification: bash -n green on all 5 edited shell files; tt/rbtd-b.Build.sh green (theurge dev profile); tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh 25/26 with rbtdrf_rv_rbrv_all_vessels pre-existing fail unchanged from BBAA3/BBAA4/BBAAs baseline (carrying-forward marshal-zero validator surfacing); tt/rbw-tf.QualifyFast.sh passes — tabtarget structure, RBW colophon registrations, and generated context freshness all green. No new shellcheck warnings expected (pattern-mechanical refactor, no new variable patterns or constructs introduced). Empirical re-run of onboarding-sequence pending operator go-ahead per docket iteration loop. Caveat: this sweep covers the orchestrator-passes-args set; if a handler outside that set (e.g., bash-level callers in handbook prose using positional args to a `""`-channel tabtarget) was relying on the old silent-pass-through, it will now warn-and-clear and fail downstream. Discovery recipe for residual sites: grep for `${1:-}` reads in rbg*/rbf*/rbh*/rbl*/rbr*/rbo*/rb[a-z]_*.sh handler functions and cross-reference against zipper channel column for `""`. Out of scope for this notch (deferred per docket): full project-wide audit of every `""`-channel handler's `${1:-}`/`${2:-}` reads to flush remaining latent failures before they surface empirically.

48abbe1e  ₢BBAAs    fix off-by-one in zrbfd_enshrine_extract_anchors after BBAA3 preflight prepended buildStepOutputs[0]. Empirical post-curl-primitive onboarding-sequence run (5/6 PASSED before stop) hit `[rbfd_enshrine] ERROR: Empty decoded buildStepOutputs` on ordain-airgap's enshrine of rbev-bottle-ifrit-forge. The Cloud Build itself was fine — submitted, queued 8 polls, working 3 polls, SUCCESS at poll 12, 19s wall clock; build 74cd601d-962c-41d1-8961-7483ae3967bf in canc-d-canest2100003. Direct gcloud describe of the build's results.buildStepOutputs revealed ["", "<base64>"] — slot 0 empty, slot 1 the actual enshrine-copy JSON {"slot_1":{"anchor":"rbi_es/rust-slim-bookworm-caaf9ca7ac:rust-slim-bookworm-caaf9ca7ac","origin":"rust:slim-bookworm","digest":"sha256:caaf9ca7ac..."}}. Root cause: BBAA3 (dca68895) prepended a step 0 'reliquary-preflight' to every ordain-path CB job; preflight does HEAD checks only and writes nothing to /builder/outputs/output, so its buildStepOutputs entry is empty string. The host-side enshrine extractor at rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh:1040 was written pre-BBAA3 when enshrine-copy was step 0 and reads buildStepOutputs[0]: jq -r '.results.buildStepOutputs[0] // empty' followed by base64 decode and `test -s` on the decoded file — which now decodes empty-string-base64 to empty-file and trips the 'Empty decoded buildStepOutputs' die at line 1046. Asymmetric evidence: the parallel conjure parser at rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh:1264 already reads buildStepOutputs[1] (conjure has always had a step preceding its hallmark step); BBAA3's 'every ordain-path CB job now starts with preflight' lift silently aligned the four entry points to that pattern, and only the conjure parser was already at [1] — the enshrine parser stayed at [0] and was guaranteed to fail on first post-BBAA3 empirical enshrine. Same 'first empirical run after BBAA3 preflight landing' bug class previously surfaced as 9ea3b9fa (CB automapSubstitutions rejecting bash default-value syntax in preflight script), 33798bfc (alpine missing /bin/bash), 8c057a45 (3-curl-fail orchestrator-kill that motivated the curl primitive); same dca68895 deferral-to-BBAAs precedent. Edit shape: line 1040 buildStepOutputs[0]→buildStepOutputs[1]; line 1036 comment expanded from 'buildStepOutputs[0] is base64-encoded JSON from the enshrine step' to 'buildStepOutputs[1] is base64-encoded JSON from the enshrine-copy step; step 0 is the in-pool reliquary preflight, which does not write /builder/outputs/output' — load-bearing per CLAUDE.md (without the WHY a future reader sees an arbitrary-looking [1] and could 'fix' back to [0]). Operator chose minimal over robust: the more defensive variant `[.results.buildStepOutputs[]? | select(. != "")] | first // empty` was discussed and deferred — would decouple parser from preflight-step positioning at the cost of breaking the implicit 'step 1 is enshrine-copy' positional contract; minimal preserves symmetry with the line 1264 conjure parser (also positional [1]) and bounds future-shuffling cost to a one-line review of two paired sites. Sweep follow-up not done this notch and worth tracking: grep buildStepOutputs[0-9]+ across the codebase to confirm bind/graft/about/vouch parsers don't have parallel off-by-one fingerprints (dca68895's docket gap explicitly flagged that standalone vouch and standalone about are reached by reliquary-preflight via conjure/bind/graft flow but were not enumerated in the four direct entry points). Verification: bash -n green on rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh; tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh 25/26 with rbtdrf_rv_rbrv_all_vessels pre-existing fail unchanged from BBAA3/BBAA4/BBAAs baseline — no new failures from this notch; empirical re-run of onboarding-sequence pending operator decision since per docket iteration loop the only sanctioned action after fix-recommend is await guidance, which was given as 'apply minimal fix and notch'.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                
8c057a45  ₢BBAAs    BCG-conformant build-poll diagnostics + extract rbgu_http_request primitive. Empirical onboarding-sequence run after the alpine-shebang fix surfaced a new failure mode in conjure-srjcl: 3 consecutive curl failures at poll 131 killed the orchestrator while the underlying Cloud Build was healthy and still running (build d033f203 SUCCESS at 17:26:23Z; all 5 SLSA artifacts landed in GAR). Forensic gap surfaced: bare '-s' on the poll-loop curl swallowed stderr entirely, '${z_curl_rc}' was captured into a local but never logged anywhere, and four loop-internal temp files (ZRBFC_BUILD_STATUS_FILE, ZRBFC_STATUS_CHECK_FILE, the literal rbfc_poll_err_check.txt, and the missing curl-stderr file) were single-named-overwriting on every attempt — violating BCG §'In loops, use an auto-incrementing integer for uniqueness' (BCG line 536-553). Survey of curl callsites found three convergent patterns (rbgu_http_json, zrbgv_http_get_with_5xx_retry, rbgo_exchange_jwt_capture) that all share the BCG-conformant inline shape: '-sS', stderr-to-temp-file, surface rc + stderr-file in warn, but each module reimplements the curl flag boilerplate inline. Discussed extracting a primitive helper now vs continuing to inline — went with helper extraction (Option C) since rbfc would otherwise be the 4th inline site, and a follow-up pace will port rbgu_http_json+zrbgv_http_get_with_5xx_retry to the helper for DRY treatment. Helper rbgu_http_request lives in rbgu_Utility.sh adjacent to rbgu_http_json (rbgu kindle is already a dependency of rbfd_cli before zrbfc_kindle, verified). Signature: method/url/token/resp_file/code_file/stderr_file/[body_file] — method-parameterized so future port is mechanical; hardcoded Authorization/Accept headers and conditional Content-Type for body cases (no array param needed since the three convergent callsites have header sets derivable from {method, token, has-body}; bash 3.2 array-as-parameter awkwardness avoided per BCG). Returns curl exit rc — caller decides policy. Refactored zrbfc_wait_build_completion: per-poll-attempt files numbered by z_polls (already a loop counter, no new variable per BCG Exception 2); failure-branch warnings now surface 'rc=${z_curl_rc}' and 'see ${z_stderr_file}' (mirrors rbgv:140 wording exactly); added buc_log_pipe of stderr file content into transcript; tracks z_last_good_response and copies to ZRBFC_BUILD_STATUS_FILE at loop exit (rbgu's 'register winner under bare infix' pattern at rbgu:357-359). External consumers of ZRBFC_BUILD_STATUS_FILE in rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh:1040,1264 unaffected — bare-name contract preserved. Removed unused ZRBFC_STATUS_CHECK_FILE kindle constant (no external consumers). Tolerance at z_max_consecutive_failures=3 unchanged — Pass-1 collects rc data; if next empirical failure surfaces a real >60s outage, Pass-2 calibrates tolerance on observed rc distribution rather than guessing. Behavior delta on rbfc poll: now sends 'Accept: application/json' header it previously omitted (Cloud Build REST API returns JSON regardless of Accept; benign canonicalization). Verification: bash -n green on both files; tt/rbtd-b.Build.sh green; tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh 25/26 (rbtdrf_rv_rbrv_all_vessels pre-existing fail unchanged from BBAA3/BBAA4 baseline); shellcheck warnings on both files are all pre-existing (kindle vars consumed cross-module, $(<file) on local declarations) — no new warnings from this notch. 33798bfc  ₢BBAAs    fix in-pool reliquary preflight shebang for alpine: switch from #!/bin/bash to #!/bin/sh in both the source script (rbgjr01-reliquary-preflight.sh:1) and the preflight step assembly (zrbfc_assemble_preflight_step in rbfc_FoundryCore.sh:551, which strips line 1 of the source and re-prepends a shebang). Empirical onboarding-sequence run after the substitution-syntax fix surfaced a new Cloud Build failure: step 0 'reliquary-preflight' on the alpine reliquary image exited 255 with 'exec script: no such file or directory' — the classic missing-interpreter ENOENT, here for /bin/bash since alpine ships busybox sh only and bash is not installed. Convention check across the 14 step scripts in rbgj* tree (rbgja*, rbgjb*, rbgje*, rbgji*, rbgjm*, rbgjr*, rbgjv*) showed an established precedent: 13 of 14 scripts use #!/bin/bash and run on docker/gcloud images that ship bash; the one outlier rbgjv01-download-verifier.sh uses #!/bin/sh and runs on the alpine reliquary tool image — wired via the explicit shell column 'sh' in the rbfc_FoundryCore.sh:469 step-defs table. The new preflight broke the pattern by advertising bash while running on alpine. Edit #2 (the assembly's printf) is the load-bearing one — the assembly's tail -n +2 strips the source shebang anyway, so the source line never reaches Cloud Build. Edit #1 (the source shebang) keeps the file consistent with the rbgjv01 precedent that source shebang matches runtime shell. Script body is already POSIX-compatible: test, case, for, ${VAR%:*}, ${VAR##*:}, printf, grep/cut, [ -z ], >&2; the only mild non-POSIX is set -euo pipefail which busybox ash has supported since 1.27 (2017) and modern alpine ships >=1.36. 7167b795  ₢BBAAs    Sweep rbw-* zipper channels from "" to "param1" and refactor handlers to read BUZ_FOLIO for the 14 enrollments whose orchestrator/operator-facing call shape passes a positional folio (vessel/hallmark/locator/stamp). Empirical post-BBAAs onboarding-sequence run (5/8 PASSED) hit `rbfd_enshrine: Vessel argument required (sigil or path)` on ordain-airgap's enshrine of rbev-bottle-ifrit-forge, with the diagnostic `WARNING: Colophon 'rbw-dE' takes no folio; ignoring unexpected argument(s): rbev-bottle-ifrit-forge` confirming the dispatcher stripped the arg before rbfd_enshrine's `${1:-}` could read it. Root cause: concurrent commit 17b3f85e (₢A-AAj, landed 2026-05-02 12:28:38 mid-fixture-run) tightened buz_exec_lookup's empty-channel branch from silent-pass-through to warn-and-clear ("Friendly-error UX extension: empty-channel commands now warn-and-clear when given unexpected positional args instead of silently passing them through to the formulary") to surface bogus args to truly-no-folio commands like buw-rnl. The change was correct in intent but unsynchronized: many rbw-* enrollments declared channel `""` while their handlers in rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh, rbfl_FoundryLedger.sh, rbfr_FoundryRetriever.sh, and rbfc_FoundryCore.sh read `${1:-}` for a folio they had been receiving via the prior silent-pass-through. ordain-conjure (case 4) passed because it ran at 12:03:45 — pre-17b3f85e — under the old behavior; enshrine-upstream ran at 12:30:13 — post-17b3f85e — under the new behavior and died on empty `${1:-}`. Fix shape mirrors the prior yoke fix (commit 4097610f, BBAAs): change channel `""` to `"param1"` in zipper enrollment, change handler's folio read from `${1:-}` to `${BUZ_FOLIO:-}`, and (for handlers with positional --force flag) shift the flag read from `${2:-}` to `${1:-}` since the dispatcher now consumes the folio and forwards remaining args. Affected colophons (orchestrator-passes-args set, identified via grep of rbtdri_invoke_global call sites in Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrc_crucible.rs and rbtdro_onboarding.rs): rbw-dE (rbfd_enshrine, vessel), rbw-fO (rbfd_ordain, vessel), rbw-fk (rbfd_kludge, vessel), rbw-fA (rbfl_abjure, hallmark + --force), rbw-fs (rbfr_summon, hallmark), rbw-fpf/rbw-fpc (rbfc_plumb_full/compact, hallmark), rbw-irh/rbw-irr (rbfl_rekon_hallmark/reliquary, hallmark/stamp), rbw-iwh/rbw-iwr/rbw-iwe (rbfl_wrest, locator), rbw-iJh/rbw-iJr/rbw-iJe (rbfl_jettison, locator + --force) — 14 enrollments, 11 distinct handlers (3 wrest + 3 jettison enrollments share their handlers). Excluded from sweep (genuinely no-folio, channel `""` correct): rbw-aM mantle, rbw-arr/rbw-adr roster, rbw-dL/dU/dl depot ops, rbw-dI inscribe, rbw-gPI/PE/PR/q payor, rbw-Occ/Ocr/Ocd/Ofc/Ots/Odf/Oda/Odb/Odg/Op/Og/o onboarding handbooks, rbw-iah/iar/iae audit, rbw-MZ/MP/MG marshal, rbw-ft tally, rbw-fV vouch — these handlers take no folio and the new warn-and-clear behavior catches operator typos correctly. BCG note on furnish: unlike the rbfl_yoke fix which moved zrbrv_kindle to rbfl_cli.sh's furnish per BCG "kindle is CLI-only" rule (yoke needed to write to a kindle constant RBRV_RELIQUARY before validation), the handlers in this sweep call `zrbfc_resolve_vessel`/`zrbfc_load_vessel` (pure source, no kindle) or consume hallmark/locator/stamp strings (no regime state at all), so reading BUZ_FOLIO from the function body is BCG-conformant and no CLI furnish changes are needed in rbfd_cli.sh, rbfl_cli.sh, rbfr_cli.sh, or rbfc_cli.sh. The dispatcher's `BUZ_FOLIO="${z_folio}" exec ...` (buz_zipper.sh:280) makes the folio available as an inherited environment variable to the function body. Tabtarget context regenerated via tt/rbw-MG.MarshalGenerate.sh — the Folio column for the 14 affected rows flips from `—` (empty channel) to `param1`, satisfying the qualify-fast freshness gate. Verification: bash -n green on all 5 edited shell files; tt/rbtd-b.Build.sh green (theurge dev profile); tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh 25/26 with rbtdrf_rv_rbrv_all_vessels pre-existing fail unchanged from BBAA3/BBAA4/BBAAs baseline (carrying-forward marshal-zero validator surfacing); tt/rbw-tf.QualifyFast.sh passes — tabtarget structure, RBW colophon registrations, and generated context freshness all green. No new shellcheck warnings expected (pattern-mechanical refactor, no new variable patterns or constructs introduced). Empirical re-run of onboarding-sequence pending operator go-ahead per docket iteration loop. Caveat: this sweep covers the orchestrator-passes-args set; if a handler outside that set (e.g., bash-level callers in handbook prose using positional args to a `""`-channel tabtarget) was relying on the old silent-pass-through, it will now warn-and-clear and fail downstream. Discovery recipe for residual sites: grep for `${1:-}` reads in rbg*/rbf*/rbh*/rbl*/rbr*/rbo*/rb[a-z]_*.sh handler functions and cross-reference against zipper channel column for `""`. Out of scope for this notch (deferred per docket): full project-wide audit of every `""`-channel handler's `${1:-}`/`${2:-}` reads to flush remaining latent failures before they surface empirically.                    
768cc71c  ₣BB       BCG: codify executable-bit rule — _cli.sh must be +x, all other module *.sh must not. Adds Filesystem marker subsection to § CLI as Module Gateway, drops 'executable' from the line 41 file-map (now stated separately as a hard rule), and adds matching item to Module Maturity Checklist §                            
48abbe1e  ₢BBAAs    fix off-by-one in zrbfd_enshrine_extract_anchors after BBAA3 preflight prepended buildStepOutputs[0]. Empirical post-curl-primitive onboarding-sequence run (5/6 PASSED before stop) hit `[rbfd_enshrine] ERROR: Empty decoded buildStepOutputs` on ordain-airgap's enshrine of rbev-bottle-ifrit-forge. The Cloud Build itself was fine — submitted, queued 8 polls, working 3 polls, SUCCESS at poll 12, 19s wall clock; build 74cd601d-962c-41d1-8961-7483ae3967bf in canc-d-canest2100003. Direct gcloud describe of the build's results.buildStepOutputs revealed ["", "<base64>"] — slot 0 empty, slot 1 the actual enshrine-copy JSON {"slot_1":{"anchor":"rbi_es/rust-slim-bookworm-caaf9ca7ac:rust-slim-bookworm-caaf9ca7ac","origin":"rust:slim-bookworm","digest":"sha256:caaf9ca7ac..."}}. Root cause: BBAA3 (dca68895) prepended a step 0 'reliquary-preflight' to every ordain-path CB job; preflight does HEAD checks only and writes nothing to /builder/outputs/output, so its buildStepOutputs entry is empty string. The host-side enshrine extractor at rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh:1040 was written pre-BBAA3 when enshrine-copy was step 0 and reads buildStepOutputs[0]: jq -r '.results.buildStepOutputs[0] // empty' followed by base64 decode and `test -s` on the decoded file — which now decodes empty-string-base64 to empty-file and trips the 'Empty decoded buildStepOutputs' die at line 1046. Asymmetric evidence: the parallel conjure parser at rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh:1264 already reads buildStepOutputs[1] (conjure has always had a step preceding its hallmark step); BBAA3's 'every ordain-path CB job now starts with preflight' lift silently aligned the four entry points to that pattern, and only the conjure parser was already at [1] — the enshrine parser stayed at [0] and was guaranteed to fail on first post-BBAA3 empirical enshrine. Same 'first empirical run after BBAA3 preflight landing' bug class previously surfaced as 9ea3b9fa (CB automapSubstitutions rejecting bash default-value syntax in preflight script), 33798bfc (alpine missing /bin/bash), 8c057a45 (3-curl-fail orchestrator-kill that motivated the curl primitive); same dca68895 deferral-to-BBAAs precedent. Edit shape: line 1040 buildStepOutputs[0]→buildStepOutputs[1]; line 1036 comment expanded from 'buildStepOutputs[0] is base64-encoded JSON from the enshrine step' to 'buildStepOutputs[1] is base64-encoded JSON from the enshrine-copy step; step 0 is the in-pool reliquary preflight, which does not write /builder/outputs/output' — load-bearing per CLAUDE.md (without the WHY a future reader sees an arbitrary-looking [1] and could 'fix' back to [0]). Operator chose minimal over robust: the more defensive variant `[.results.buildStepOutputs[]? | select(. != "")] | first // empty` was discussed and deferred — would decouple parser from preflight-step positioning at the cost of breaking the implicit 'step 1 is enshrine-copy' positional contract; minimal preserves symmetry with the line 1264 conjure parser (also positional [1]) and bounds future-shuffling cost to a one-line review of two paired sites. Sweep follow-up not done this notch and worth tracking: grep buildStepOutputs[0-9]+ across the codebase to confirm bind/graft/about/vouch parsers don't have parallel off-by-one fingerprints (dca68895's docket gap explicitly flagged that standalone vouch and standalone about are reached by reliquary-preflight via conjure/bind/graft flow but were not enumerated in the four direct entry points). Verification: bash -n green on rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh; tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh 25/26 with rbtdrf_rv_rbrv_all_vessels pre-existing fail unchanged from BBAA3/BBAA4/BBAAs baseline — no new failures from this notch; empirical re-run of onboarding-sequence pending operator decision since per docket iteration loop the only sanctioned action after fix-recommend is await guidance, which was given as 'apply minimal fix and notch'.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                
8c057a45  ₢BBAAs    BCG-conformant build-poll diagnostics + extract rbgu_http_request primitive. Empirical onboarding-sequence run after the alpine-shebang fix surfaced a new failure mode in conjure-srjcl: 3 consecutive curl failures at poll 131 killed the orchestrator while the underlying Cloud Build was healthy and still running (build d033f203 SUCCESS at 17:26:23Z; all 5 SLSA artifacts landed in GAR). Forensic gap surfaced: bare '-s' on the poll-loop curl swallowed stderr entirely, '${z_curl_rc}' was captured into a local but never logged anywhere, and four loop-internal temp files (ZRBFC_BUILD_STATUS_FILE, ZRBFC_STATUS_CHECK_FILE, the literal rbfc_poll_err_check.txt, and the missing curl-stderr file) were single-named-overwriting on every attempt — violating BCG §'In loops, use an auto-incrementing integer for uniqueness' (BCG line 536-553). Survey of curl callsites found three convergent patterns (rbgu_http_json, zrbgv_http_get_with_5xx_retry, rbgo_exchange_jwt_capture) that all share the BCG-conformant inline shape: '-sS', stderr-to-temp-file, surface rc + stderr-file in warn, but each module reimplements the curl flag boilerplate inline. Discussed extracting a primitive helper now vs continuing to inline — went with helper extraction (Option C) since rbfc would otherwise be the 4th inline site, and a follow-up pace will port rbgu_http_json+zrbgv_http_get_with_5xx_retry to the helper for DRY treatment. Helper rbgu_http_request lives in rbgu_Utility.sh adjacent to rbgu_http_json (rbgu kindle is already a dependency of rbfd_cli before zrbfc_kindle, verified). Signature: method/url/token/resp_file/code_file/stderr_file/[body_file] — method-parameterized so future port is mechanical; hardcoded Authorization/Accept headers and conditional Content-Type for body cases (no array param needed since the three convergent callsites have header sets derivable from {method, token, has-body}; bash 3.2 array-as-parameter awkwardness avoided per BCG). Returns curl exit rc — caller decides policy. Refactored zrbfc_wait_build_completion: per-poll-attempt files numbered by z_polls (already a loop counter, no new variable per BCG Exception 2); failure-branch warnings now surface 'rc=${z_curl_rc}' and 'see ${z_stderr_file}' (mirrors rbgv:140 wording exactly); added buc_log_pipe of stderr file content into transcript; tracks z_last_good_response and copies to ZRBFC_BUILD_STATUS_FILE at loop exit (rbgu's 'register winner under bare infix' pattern at rbgu:357-359). External consumers of ZRBFC_BUILD_STATUS_FILE in rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh:1040,1264 unaffected — bare-name contract preserved. Removed unused ZRBFC_STATUS_CHECK_FILE kindle constant (no external consumers). Tolerance at z_max_consecutive_failures=3 unchanged — Pass-1 collects rc data; if next empirical failure surfaces a real >60s outage, Pass-2 calibrates tolerance on observed rc distribution rather than guessing. Behavior delta on rbfc poll: now sends 'Accept: application/json' header it previously omitted (Cloud Build REST API returns JSON regardless of Accept; benign canonicalization). Verification: bash -n green on both files; tt/rbtd-b.Build.sh green; tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh 25/26 (rbtdrf_rv_rbrv_all_vessels pre-existing fail unchanged from BBAA3/BBAA4 baseline); shellcheck warnings on both files are all pre-existing (kindle vars consumed cross-module, $(<file) on local declarations) — no new warnings from this notch. 33798bfc  ₢BBAAs    fix in-pool reliquary preflight shebang for alpine: switch from #!/bin/bash to #!/bin/sh in both the source script (rbgjr01-reliquary-preflight.sh:1) and the preflight step assembly (zrbfc_assemble_preflight_step in rbfc_FoundryCore.sh:551, which strips line 1 of the source and re-prepends a shebang). Empirical onboarding-sequence run after the substitution-syntax fix surfaced a new Cloud Build failure: step 0 'reliquary-preflight' on the alpine reliquary image exited 255 with 'exec script: no such file or directory' — the classic missing-interpreter ENOENT, here for /bin/bash since alpine ships busybox sh only and bash is not installed. Convention check across the 14 step scripts in rbgj* tree (rbgja*, rbgjb*, rbgje*, rbgji*, rbgjm*, rbgjr*, rbgjv*) showed an established precedent: 13 of 14 scripts use #!/bin/bash and run on docker/gcloud images that ship bash; the one outlier rbgjv01-download-verifier.sh uses #!/bin/sh and runs on the alpine reliquary tool image — wired via the explicit shell column 'sh' in the rbfc_FoundryCore.sh:469 step-defs table. The new preflight broke the pattern by advertising bash while running on alpine. Edit #2 (the assembly's printf) is the load-bearing one — the assembly's tail -n +2 strips the source shebang anyway, so the source line never reaches Cloud Build. Edit #1 (the source shebang) keeps the file consistent with the rbgjv01 precedent that source shebang matches runtime shell. Script body is already POSIX-compatible: test, case, for, ${VAR%:*}, ${VAR##*:}, printf, grep/cut, [ -z ], >&2; the only mild non-POSIX is set -euo pipefail which busybox ash has supported since 1.27 (2017) and modern alpine ships >=1.36.
