c547c62a jjb:1011-755d661c:₢BBAAs:n: Narrow the prior commit's enforce-skip list to drop rbob_ordain. The split that made my prior arm too wide: ordain has TWO entry points and they live in different CLIs. rbw-fO → rbfd_ordain is vessel-keyed (foundry-direct), goes through rbfd_cli.sh, never loads a nameplate, never runs zrbrn_enforce — and it is the entry point onboarding-sequence actually invokes (RBTDRM_COLOPHON_ORDAIN = "rbw-fO" at rbtdrm_manifest.rs:30). rbw-tO → rbob_ordain is nameplate-keyed (Theurge OrdainCycle, imprint channel), goes through rbob_cli.sh, IS the gate where zrbrn_enforce earns its keep — it is the natural pre-cloud-spend regime-validity check on a nameplate-affiliated build. Putting rbob_ordain into the skip list achieved nothing for the immediate failure (the failing path is rbw-cKS → rbob_kludge_sentry, not any rbob_ordain) and silently weakened the rbw-tO validation surface. Final skip list: rbob_kludge, rbob_kludge_bottle, rbob_kludge_sentry — all three are nameplate-keyed kludge entry points that write RBRN_*_HALLMARK and self-veto on marshal-zero blank-by-design state. rbob_kludge stays in the list because it is reachable two ways: directly via rbw-tK (Theurge KludgeCycle) and indirectly via rbob_kludge_bottle's body (rbob_bottle.sh:481-484 is just zrbob_sentinel; rbob_kludge); ${z_command} is set in furnish from the zipper-registered name for the dispatched tabtarget, so all three matter. The differential foundry-kindle block at the tail of zrbob_furnish keeps rbob_ordain — ordain still needs zrbfd_kindle to perform the Cloud Build; only the enforce dispatch drops it. Build syntax-check green (bash -n); 91/91 rbtd unit tests pass; fast suite 25/26 (pre-existing rbtdrf_rv_rbrv_all_vessels unchanged from BBAAs; regime-smoke skipped under suite-level fail-fast as expected). Empirical onboarding-sequence re-run pending operator go-ahead per docket discipline.
142cf920 jjb:1011-03ca4355:₢BBAAs:n: Fix kludge/ordain self-veto on RBRN_*_HALLMARK surfaced by onboarding-sequence empirical run. Case 2 kludge-tadmor failed at the kludge-sentry sub-step with 'RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK must not be empty' — zrbrn_enforce ran unconditionally in rbob_cli.sh after zrbrn_kindle, but rbob_kludge_sentry / rbob_kludge / rbob_kludge_bottle / rbob_ordain are precisely the commands that write RBRN_*_HALLMARK; their kindle path self-vetoed on the very fields they were about to populate. Marshal-zero blanks both hallmarks by design (rblm_cli.sh:168), and rbtdrp_pristine.rs:117 explicitly catalogs them as RBTDRP_RBRN_BLANK_FIELDS — pristine state IS blank-hallmark by contract. Mirrors the yoke/RBRV self-veto pattern fixed in BBAAs:4097610f, but applied in-CLI rather than via file split: rbob_cli handles a coherent multi-command surface (kludge/ordain write hallmarks; charge/start/stop/hail/rack/scry/info/validate/charged require populated hallmarks for ZRBOB_*_IMAGE refs), so splitting like rbfl_cli vs rbrv_cli would fragment the surface. Survey of in-codebase patterns: Pattern A (file split, rbfl_cli/rbrv_cli) — not applicable. Pattern B (command-keyed differential within one CLI, already used at rbob_cli.sh:164-168 for differential zrbfd_kindle) — applied. Pattern C (rbfd_enshrine internal vessel resolution via zrbfc_resolve_vessel/zrbfc_load_vessel) — deliberately avoided per operator note that it was 'messy' in the prior outing. Replaced the unconditional zrbrn_enforce at line 152 with a case on ${z_command} parallel to the existing foundry-kindle dispatch — the four write-target commands skip enforce, all others enforce as today. Strict steady-state validator (rbw-rnv via rbrn_cli.sh) untouched — remains the path for explicit 'is this regime steady-state valid?' questions. Build syntax-check green (bash -n); 91/91 rbtd unit tests pass; fast suite 25/26 (pre-existing rbtdrf_rv_rbrv_all_vessels unchanged from BBAAs; regime-smoke skipped under suite-level fail-fast as expected). Empirical onboarding-sequence re-run pending operator go-ahead per docket discipline.
4097610f jjb:1011-9cfe5991:₢BBAAs:n: Re-channel rbw-dY (yoke) to param1 with vessel sigil as folio. Eliminates duplication with rbrv_cli.sh's existing BUZ_FOLIO block, fixes BCG kindle-from-mutator violation, and fixes the marshal-zero yoke failure surfaced by onboarding-sequence case 1. zrbrv_kindle moves from inside rbfl_yoke to the rbfl_cli.sh furnish (BCG: kindle is CLI-only). zrbrv_enforce intentionally skipped in the yoke path — yoke writes RBRV_RELIQUARY (kindle constant, min=1); pre-write enforce self-vetoes on marshal-zero state where MZ blanks RBRV_RELIQUARY by design. rbw-rvv (rbrv_cli.sh:108) remains the strict validator path. Yoke arg order flips: was <stamp> <vessel>, now <vessel> <stamp> per param1 channel convention. Updated: zipper enrollment (channel param1), rbfl_cli.sh furnish (BUZ_FOLIO-gated source+kindle, parallels rbrv_cli.sh:103-109), rbfl_yoke body (drops zrbfc_resolve/load/kindle, becomes pure consumer), rbtdro_yoke fixture caller (passes sigil not path, swaps arg order), rbhodf handbook prose, RBSDY spec, generated tabtarget context. 91/91 unit tests pass; fast suite 25/26 (pre-existing rbtdrf_rv_rbrv_all_vessels failure unchanged — that's rbw-rvv flagging post-MZ vessels as invalid by full enforcement, which is correct steady-state semantic). Smoke-tested: yoke arg validation, BUZ_FOLIO-gated vessel-not-found path.
b6a2080f jjb:1011-2fe8de61:₢BBAAe:W: Added gauntlet-canonical-establish StateProgressing fixture covering §2 of release qualification — canonical depot levy, governor mantle, retriever/director invest, each gated by an rbtdrb_Probe so a-la-carte single-case rerun fails cleanly when the prior case's state is absent. New module rbtdrk_canonical.rs (classifier 'k' for canonical), family stem 'canest' with 6-digit auto-increment moniker, canonical prefixes canc-/canr- distinct from pristine throwaway markers. iam-propagation-wait dropped from the original §2 case list during mount-time review — access-probe immediately after each invest doubles as the propagation gate, and propagation waiting is properly the tabtarget's responsibility, not a separate fixture case. Wired into rbtdrm manifest, fixture-disposition lookup, sections registry, lib.rs module + test-module declarations; new tabtarget launchers tt/rbtd-r.Run.canonical-establish.sh and tt/rbtd-s.SingleCase.canonical-establish.sh. 5 unit tests in rbtdtk_canonical.rs (prefix shape, family stem value, disposition lookup, sections registry, install-rejects-missing-rbrr). Build clean; 69/69 unit tests pass; fast TestSuite enrollment-validation 47/47, regime-validation 25/26 with the baseline marshal-zero carry-forward (orthogonal). SingleCase tabtarget verified — returns the 4 expected cases. Helper duplication across rbtdrp_pristine and rbtdrk_canonical (read_env / replace_env / git_add_and_commit / canonical_rbra / install-prefixes / install-moniker / compose-project-id / pick-next-moniker / invoke_logged) is a candidate for shared-helper extraction in a future pace; not in scope here. Mount-time discussion surfaced two follow-on items: (1) BBAAj theurge-manifest-coverage-check slated to make rbtdrm declared-colophons list runtime-enforced (positive: invoked must be declared; negative: declared must be invoked at least once on success); (2) suggested calibrant-coverage additions to BBAAh+BBAAi (or a follow-up pace pair) to prove BBAAj's checks via synthetic fixtures with mis-declared manifests. The interleaving of tabtarget invocations with fact-file reads and rbrr.env mutations — carried forward from rbtdrp_pristine — is intentional design: orchestrator-level cross-checks (RBDC compose vs payor fact, contract assertions across tabtargets) cannot be done by any single tabtarget.
eb4751fc jjb:1011-85013b94:₢BBAAY:W: Spec-only restructure of RBSRR-RegimeRepo.adoc landing BBAAQ Surface 4's depot-identity-collapse retirement at the regime spec itself. Two notches in this chat plus a third operator/parallel-chat edit. Notch 07730eba covered original docket scope: deleted axhrv_variable blocks for the three retired RBRR fields (rbrr_depot_project_id, rbrr_gar_repository, rbrr_gcb_pool_stem); added axhrgv_variable block for rbrr_depot_moniker in the Resource Prefixing group between cloud_prefix and runtime_prefix so the depot identity tuple lives together (followed file's group convention axhrgv over docket's literal axhrv); updated group intro for 'all three variables' / 'two prefixes'; rewrote line 8 prose to tuple-based identity ('fall out as RBDC constants' matches paddock vocabulary); added :rbrr_depot_moniker: mapping to RBS0:327 next to rbrr_cloud_prefix; replaced RBSGM:27 stale rbw-*-[0-9]{12} regex require with NOTE per RBSDE:38 precedent (enforced upstream by zrbrr_enforce). Notch fc815a27 followup repaired the three axho_* operations paragraphs (axhro_kindle, axhro_validate, axhro_render) which were stale on multiple counts: named retired RBRR fields by uppercase, cited rbcr_* phantom module instead of buv_* render path, listed five fictional render section names, claimed unexpected-var-in-red and validate-after-render behaviors that don't exist. Followup over-corrected by leaking BUK helper-function names (buv_*_enroll, buv_scope_sentinel, buv_lock, buv_report RBRR, buv_render RBRR) and ZRBRR_DOCKER_ENV array name into prose — operator surfaced this as discipline violation: spec language describes core ideas; code references arrive only via linked-term replacement text, not free-text bash function names. Operator/parallel-chat then applied the discipline cleanly to RBSRR axho_* paragraphs in-place, replacing bash-routine leakage with concept-language (kindle: 'Kindles the regime fields by enrolling each field with its type-specific constraints under named groups. Detects unexpected RBRR_ variables. Composes the docker environment injection set and locks the regime against further modification.'; validate: 'Validates enrolled fields against type-specific constraints from enrollment. Validates directory existence for {rbrr_vessel_dir} and {rbrr_secrets_dir}. Enforces format constraints on {rbrr_cloud_prefix} and {rbrr_depot_moniker}, and the joint-length cap on ${RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX}d-${RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER} (≤30 chars per the GCP project ID limit).'; render: 'Renders the regime grouped by section with presentation gates per group. Sections: Resource Prefixing, Vessel and Local Configuration, GCP Infrastructure, Google Cloud Build Configuration, Secrets Directory, Public Docs.'). The 30-char GCP project ID joint-length cap and operator-visible section-name list both retained as conceptual/UX commitments. Path-A absorption of the discipline issue: BBAAZ docket reslated to add 'Companion sweep: RBSRR axho_* prose discipline' section plus verification line (grep -E 'buv_|ZRBRR_' RBSRR returns zero hits) plus reference back to fc815a27 — gives BBAAZ executor authority to sweep any future regression and applies the same discipline to the new procedural specs being authored there at RBSDE-as-gold-standard depth tier. BBAAZ remains rough; operator decision was 'fix in updated work of BBAAZ' rather than reverting fc815a27, so commit-history additive trail of the discipline conversation preserved. RBSRR now textually consistent with rbrr_regime.sh implementation as of HEAD: regime spec is source-of-truth for the (RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX, RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER) tuple, the three retired fields are gone everywhere they were definition sites, RBS0 mapping table reflects current regime shape, RBSGM mantle procedure no longer carries stale timestamp-shape regex, and operations prose describes kindle/validate/render at concept level with linked-term field references throughout.
fc815a27 jjb:1011-07730eba:₢BBAAY:n: Repair the three stale axhro_* operations prose paragraphs in RBSRR-RegimeRepo.adoc that the previous BBAAY notch (07730eba) deliberately surfaced as itch candidates under strict docket discipline. Operator instruction was 'just do it if it is clear now and notch' — follow-up confirmed all three were unambiguously stale by visible-code contradiction, not just retired-field references that happened to live nearby. Inspecting code revealed the staleness was deeper than the original two surfaced lines: axhro_kindle (line 167-169) described an implementation generation that predates the BUK validation framework adoption (no longer uses VAR=${VAR:-} default-only kindle pattern, no compgen prefix scan, no ZRBRR_ROLLUP serialization string — confirmed via grep across rbrr_regime.sh and full project tree). Actual zrbrr_kindle in rbrr_regime.sh:30-74 enrolls fields via buv_string_enroll / buv_ipv4_enroll / buv_gname_enroll / buv_decimal_enroll under buv_group_enroll-named groups, runs buv_scope_sentinel RBRR RBRR_ for unexpected-var detection, composes ZRBRR_DOCKER_ENV array (line 69) for docker -e injection, and seals via buv_lock RBRR (line 73). Replacement prose: 'Enrolls fields via buv_*_enroll type-specific helpers under named groups. Detects unexpected RBRR_ variables via buv_scope_sentinel. Composes ZRBRR_DOCKER_ENV injection array and locks the regime.' Three lines, accurate, framework-truthful. axhro_validate (line 174-177) was stale on three counts: (1) named retired RBRR fields DEPOT_PROJECT_ID and GAR_REPOSITORY in 'Checks required fields' enumeration despite BBAAM/BBAAQ retiring both; (2) cited 'buv_env_* type checkers' which is not the actual API — type checking is implicit in buv_<TYPE>_enroll registration consumed by buv_vet/buv_report; (3) had separate 'Validates machine type and timeout format constraints' line that is just a subset of the type-checker contract not warranting standalone callout. Actual entry point: rbrr_cli.sh:34 calls buv_report RBRR (which delegates to buv_vet for the type-constraint checks); rbrr_regime.sh:83-90 zrbrr_enforce adds buv_dir_exists for RBRR_VESSEL_DIR and RBRR_SECRETS_DIR plus the BBAAM-era format checks (lines 94-95 DEPOT_MONIKER regex, lines 97-98 CLOUD_PREFIX regex) and joint-length cap (lines 103-109) for the 30-char GCP project ID limit. Replacement prose: 'Validates enrolled fields via buv_report RBRR against type-specific constraints from enrollment. Validates directory existence for RBRR_VESSEL_DIR and RBRR_SECRETS_DIR. Enforces RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER and RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX format and their joint-length cap (≤30 chars for GCP project ID limit).' Three lines, structured around what zrbrr_enforce actually does. axhro_render (line 182-185) was stale on four counts: (1) cited 'rbcr_* shared module' as the render path — grep across project shows no rbcr_* function namespace in active use; actual render is buv_render in BUK (rbrr_cli.sh:43 calls buv_render RBRR with title); (2) listed 5 sections 'GCP Infrastructure, Artifact Registry, Service Account Credentials, Container Runtime Configuration, Build Configuration' that don't match any of the 6 actual buv_group_enroll calls in zrbrr_kindle (Resource Prefixing, Vessel and Local Configuration, GCP Infrastructure, Google Cloud Build Configuration, Secrets Directory, Public Docs); (3) 'Displays unexpected variables in red' is wrong placement — unexpected-var detection happens at kindle time via buv_scope_sentinel, not render time; (4) 'Validates after full render completes' is wrong — render and validate are separate passes via different cli commands (rbrr_cli.sh:30 rbrr_validate vs rbrr_cli.sh:39 rbrr_render). buv_render at buv_validation.sh:789-829 walks z_buv_*_roll arrays, applies group-level gates via zbuv_group_gate_recite, displays via bupr_ presentation registry; no validation, no in-line error coloring of unexpected vars. Replacement prose: 'Renders enrolled fields via buv_render RBRR with group structure and presentation gates. Sections: Resource Prefixing, Vessel and Local Configuration, GCP Infrastructure, Google Cloud Build Configuration, Secrets Directory, Public Docs.' Two lines, accurate, dropped the two incorrect bullets. Verification: grep against repaired file for stale tokens (DEPOT_PROJECT_ID|GAR_REPOSITORY|GCB_POOL_STEM|compgen|ZRBRR_ROLLUP|rbcr_|Artifact Registry|Service Account Credentials|Container Runtime Configuration) returns single legitimate hit at line 32 which is the new RBDC_* derived-constants list in the depot_moniker variable block (intentional, accurate). All three axhro_* paragraphs now describe the BUK-framework-era implementation truthfully. Scope-extension boundary: this notch follows the same RBSRR file as the previous BBAAY notch (07730eba), so this is a structural continuation of the same pace, not new scope. Itch candidates from the previous wrap report fully repaired. No further itch surfaces remain in RBSRR — file textually consistent with rbrr_regime.sh implementation as of HEAD.
07730eba jjb:1011-3a4ae2a5:₢BBAAY:n: Land BBAAQ Surface 4's depot-identity-collapse retirement at RBSRR (the regime spec defining rbrr.env). Carved out of BBAAV at mount time when the scope contradiction surfaced — BBAAV's docket said 'no behavioral or structural changes to spec content beyond xref flipping', but RBSRR's references to the three retired RBRR fields are definition sites, not xrefs. RBSRR-RegimeRepo.adoc: deleted three axhrv_variable definition blocks for the retired fields ({rbrr_depot_project_id}, {rbrr_gar_repository}, {rbrr_gcb_pool_stem}); added one axhrgv_variable block for the new {rbrr_depot_moniker} field (placed in the Resource Prefixing group between {rbrr_cloud_prefix} and {rbrr_runtime_prefix} so the depot identity tuple lives together — followed file's group convention axhrgv over docket's literal axhrv since cloud_prefix uses axhrgv); updated group intro at lines 13-16 from 'Operator-chosen prefixes' / 'Both variables must be populated' / 'The prefix must be 2-11 characters' to 'Operator-chosen identifiers' / 'All three variables must be populated' / 'The two prefixes must be 2-11 characters' since the moniker has a different format (^[a-z][a-z0-9]*$, length 1-26) than the prefix format (^[a-z][a-z0-9-]*-$); updated intro pr

content.text:
You're right. `--force` was unauthorized — a prior agent's invention.

## Origin trace

**Earliest appearance**: docket revision `260128-2116` for ₢APAAT (then-silks `add-image-delete-by-ark`):

> "Use GAR API or gcloud to delete specific tags ... Confirm before deletion **or require --force flag**."

That was the seed. From there it propagated mechanically:

1. **₢APAAT docket evolution** (Jan-Feb 2026): subsequent reslating preserved `--force` across multiple revisions (silks rotated from `add-image-delete-by-ark` → `implement-rbf-delete-single-tag`); each reslate kept the line because it was already in the docket from the previous revision.

2. **₢APAAT alignment commit** `525b7fac` (Feb 9, marked `:A:`): "Align rbf_delete impl (add confirmation/force) and RBSID spec." A docket-only edit that pulled "(add confirmation/force)" into the spec to match the docket.

3. **Implementation commit** `bd344f92` (same day, by a Haiku sub-agent): "Rewrite rbf_delete for locator input with buc_require confirmation, update rbf_abjure to buc_require." Mechanically followed the docket — added `local z_force="${2:-}"`, `--force` doc-param, `z_skip_confirm` branching.

4. **Extract** `388dd454` (Apr 1): moved into `rbfl_FoundryLedger.sh`. `--force` preserved.

5. **BBAAs** `7167b795` (today): `${2:-}` → `${1:-}` shift specifically because the dispatcher consumes the folio. The shift exists to keep `--force` working post-channel-change.

The classic "CLI tools usually have --force" prior baked in by an early agent, never questioned, propagated through five rounds of refactor including the one we're untangling now. None of the commits show evidence of you authorizing it — the docket genealogy points back to a single Haiku-tier session interpreting "Confirm before deletion" as "Confirm before deletion **or require --force flag**" without prompting.

## Implication for the dispatcher fix

`--force` isn't load-bearing. Confirmation prompts already exist (`buc_require`); `--force` just shaves a keystroke at the cost of carrying a non-orthogonal alternate path through the function. Removing it:

- `rbfl_jettison` and `rbfl_abjure` drop `local z_force=...`, `z_skip_confirm`, the `--force` doc-param, and the `if test "${z_force}" = "--force"` branch. Confirmation always runs.
- BBAAs's `${2:-}` → `${1:-}` shift becomes irrelevant — there's no $2 to read.
- The dispatcher case-statement simplification I proposed becomes even cleaner: no post-folio positional flags exist, so the "ensure folio is at z_args[0]" invariant is the WHOLE story. No edge cases.

**Other --force callsites to audit**: my grep showed --force only in `rbfl_FoundryLedger.sh:328,346,399,405,446` (jettison + abjure). Two functions, six lines. Mechanical removal.

**Related question worth raising**: are there OTHER unauthorized variant flags I should sweep for while I'm here? The same propagation pattern (early-agent inference → docket → implementation) could have seeded other "standard CLI features" elsewhere. Quick `--` flag inventory:

content.text:
Clean — `--force` is the only `--flag` doc-param in the entire rbk tree. Two sites, both in `rbfl_FoundryLedger.sh` (jettison, abjure). No other unauthorized flag-variants surfaced.

## Refined proposal

The combined nudge becomes one notch:

**A. Dispatcher simplification** in `Tools/buk/buz_zipper.sh` — the case-statement-only model (folio normalized to `z_args[0]`; BUZ_FOLIO derived as projection):

```bash
case "${z_buz_channel_roll[z_found]}" in
    "")
      if (( ${#z_args[@]} )); then
        buc_warn "Colophon '${z_colophon}' takes no folio; ignoring unexpected argument(s): ${z_args[*]}"
        z_args=()
      fi
      ;;
    "imprint")
      z_args=("${BURD_TOKEN_3}" "${z_args[@]}")
      ;;
    "param1")
      ;;
    *)
      buc_die "buz_exec_lookup: unknown channel: ${z_buz_channel_roll[z_found]}"
      ;;
esac
BUZ_FOLIO="${z_args[0]:-}" exec "${z_base_dir}/${z_buz_module_roll[z_found]}" "${z_buz_command_roll[z_found]}" ${z_args[@]+"${z_args[@]}"}
```

**B. Handler revert** for the 14 BBAAs handlers — `${BUZ_FOLIO:-}` → `${1:-}`. Mechanical reverse of `7167b795`'s handler-side edits.

**C. Remove `--force`** from `rbfl_jettison` and `rbfl_abjure`:
- Drop `local z_force="${1:-}"` (post-revert it'd be `${2:-}`, but not needed at all).
- Drop `buc_doc_param "--force" "..."`.
- Drop `local z_skip_confirm=false` and the `if test "${z_force}" = "--force"` branch.
- Confirmation prompt (`buc_require`) always runs unconditionally.

C is what makes B clean — without --force, no handler has post-folio positional args, so BBAAs's `${2:-}` → `${1:-}` shift has nothing to revert. The handler signature collapses to "read $1 for folio, period."

## Notch shape and scope

This grows the patch from "small dispatcher fix" to "dispatcher fix + BBAAs partial revert + unauthorized-flag scrub." All three are mechanical and inter-connected. Single coronet seems right (continues the BBAAs empirical-loop's iteration shape).

Suggested commit subject sketch: "Refine dispatcher: case-statement normalizes z_args[0] to folio; BUZ_FOLIO derives from $1 projection. Revert BBAAs handler reads to positional. Strip unauthorized --force flag from rbfl_{jettison,abjure} (origin: ₢APAAT 260128-2116 docket revision, never operator-authorized; not load-bearing — confirmation prompt remains as the single coherent path)."

## Spec/handbook touch

- `RBSAA-ark_abjure.adoc` — drop `--force` from operation spec if it appears
- `RBSIJ-image_jettison.adoc` — same
- Generated tabtarget context (`tt/rbw-MG.MarshalGenerate.sh` regen) — Folio column unchanged for these (still param1)

I'll grep those specs in the actual edit pass before committing.

Awaiting guidance to proceed with the edits, or to refine further before touching files.

jjfp_repo() {
  zjjfp_sentinel

  local -r z_host="${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"
  local -r z_pubkey_path="${1:-}"
  test -n "${z_host}" || buc_die "jjfp_repo: no host (BUZ_FOLIO empty)"

  buc_doc_brief "Phase 2: Authorize curia access, clone repos, install BUK"
  buc_doc_param "${JJFP_pubkey_param}" "Path to curia SSH public key (required for remote hosts, omit for localhost)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  buc_step "Phase 2: Setting up repos on ${z_host}"

  # Discover git origin URL
  local -r z_origin_file="${ZJJFP_TEMP_PREFIX}origin.txt"
  local -r z_origin_stderr="${ZJJFP_TEMP_PREFIX}origin_stderr.txt"
  git -C "${BURD_TOOLS_DIR}/.." remote get-url origin > "${z_origin_file}" 2>"${z_origin_stderr}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to get git origin URL from curia repo — see ${z_origin_stderr}"
  local -r z_curia_origin=$(<"${z_origin_file}")
  test -n "${z_curia_origin}" || buc_die "Git origin URL is empty"
  buc_log_args "Curia origin: ${z_curia_origin}"

  if test "${z_host}" = "localhost"; then
    # Localhost: clone from local repo, direct SSH to test accounts
    local -r z_curia_root_file="${ZJJFP_TEMP_PREFIX}curia_root.txt"
    (cd "${BURD_TOOLS_DIR}/.." && pwd) > "${z_curia_root_file}" \
      || buc_die "Failed to resolve curia repo root"

content.text:
I categorized every BUZ_FOLIO read in the tree. Two distinct populations:

## Intimate consumers — `*_cli.sh` furnishes only

These read BUZ_FOLIO **before** the command function runs — at module-load/kindle time — to decide which regime/profile/nameplate file to source. Positional args aren't usable here because the furnish runs before any command-arg parsing:

| File | Use | Lines |
|------|-----|-------|
| `rbob_cli.sh` | nameplate moniker → kindle RBRN_* | 137 |
| `rbrv_cli.sh` | vessel sigil → kindle RBRV_* | 103-104 |
| `rbrn_cli.sh` | nameplate moniker → kindle RBRN_* | 202-203 |
| `rbfl_cli.sh` | vessel sigil → kindle RBRV_* | 79-80 |
| `rbra_cli.sh` | role name → kindle RBRA | 53 |
| `rbob_cli.sh` (path-listing branch) | listing on no-folio | 137-146 |
| `burn_cli.sh` | node profile → kindle | 99-100 |
| `burp_cli.sh` | investiture profile → kindle | 99-100 |
| `apcc_cli.sh` | fixture / input dir | 114, 146, 206 |
| `jjfp_cli.sh` | documented as host (target) | 32 |

These cannot be reduced to "workbench rewrites z_args" — the kindle layer needs the folio as a side-channel that's available without consuming the positional-arg slot reserved for the command-name dispatch.

## Workbench-mappable consumers — non-cli modules

Every non-cli read is a single-line `local z_x="${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"` at handler entry. **Pure equivalent of `${1:-}` if the dispatcher places the folio at `z_args[0]`:**

| File | Function | Was BBAAs? |
|------|----------|------------|
| `rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh:853` | rbfd_enshrine | yes |
| `rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh:1102` | rbfd_ordain | yes |
| `rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh:1345` | rbfd_kludge | yes |
| `rbfl_FoundryLedger.sh:198,322,393,593,660,727` | jettison/abjure/wrest/rekon (×6) | yes |
| `rbfr_FoundryRetriever.sh:57` | rbfr_summon | yes |
| `rbfc_FoundryCore.sh:1461,1473` | plumb_full / plumb_compact | yes |
| `rbgg_Governor.sh:531,582,769,783` | invest/divest retriever/director | pre-BBAAs |
| `jjfp_fundus.sh:434,515,535` | jjfp_repo / scenario / single | pre-BBAAs |
| `buhw_windows.sh:216` | buhw_environment_wsl | pre-BBAAs |

Note: rbgg / jjfp / buhw set the precedent that BBAAs extended. Same pattern existed pre-sweep.

## Answer to your question

**Both are happening in the codebase right now.** The cli.sh furnish layer uses BUZ_FOLIO intimately (kindle requires it as side-channel). The module-handler layer uses BUZ_FOLIO **redundantly** — it could be entirely replaced by the workbench-injects-folio-at-$1 pattern.

The clean two-tier model:

- **Layer 1 (dispatcher → cli.sh furnish)**: BUZ_FOLIO is the kindle side-channel. Set by dispatcher, read by `*_cli.sh` furnishes. Single role: "tell the kindle what regime to source."
- **Layer 2 (cli.sh dispatch → handler functions)**: positional `$1` is the operand. Read by handlers. Single role: "the operand for this call."

Today the layers are conflated — non-cli handlers reach into the env-var side-channel instead of taking their operand positionally. That's the "non-load-bearing variant" — same value, two access paths, intermittent collisions when one process's BUZ_FOLIO carries different semantics than another's expects (the rbob → rbfd bug).

## Refined proposal

**Do all three sweeps in one notch:**

**A. Dispatcher case-norm** in `Tools/buk/buz_zipper.sh` — z_args[0] is always the folio (or empty). BUZ_FOLIO = `${z_args[0]:-}`.

**B. Non-cli handler sweep** — every `local z_x="${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"` in non-cli modules → `local z_x="${1:-}"`. Covers BBAAs additions + the older rbgg/jjfp/buhw precedent. Result: handlers all follow one shape (positional read), no scattered exceptions.

**C. Strip --force** — `rbfl_jettison`, `rbfl_abjure`. Six lines deleted; confirmation prompt is the single coherent path.

After these three edits, BUZ_FOLIO has exactly one consumer population: `*_cli.sh` furnishes. The variable's role collapses to "kindle side-channel for cli.sh." Module handlers no longer touch it. The conceptual model is clean: dispatcher decides folio per channel → injects to z_args[0] + sets BUZ_FOLIO env → cli.sh furnish kindles from BUZ_FOLIO → handler reads $1.

**Audit recipe for verification after edits**:
```
grep -rn "BUZ_FOLIO" Tools/ | grep -v "_cli\.sh\|buz_zipper\|claude-context\|buk-claude"
```
Should return zero non-cli-furnish reads. If anything else surfaces, it's a missed site.

Awaiting guidance.

message.content: Ah thank you for discovering _FOLIO is already plumbed through.  If we rely on that, does this result in fewer pattern permutations?

content.text:
Yes — significantly fewer permutations. The rely-on-plumbing path collapses to ONE read pattern, ONE write pattern, two callsites at different scopes.

## Permutation count compared

**Today (broken)**:
- Read patterns: handlers read BUZ_FOLIO; cli.sh furnishes read BUZ_FOLIO. (Same.)
- Write patterns: dispatcher env-sets via exec.
- Bug: rbob internal callers pass `$1` while target reads BUZ_FOLIO. **One mismatch site.**

**Dispatcher-norm proposal (my prior plan)**:
- Read patterns: handlers read `$1`; cli.sh furnishes read BUZ_FOLIO. **Two patterns** (one per layer).
- Write patterns: dispatcher injects folio in BOTH `z_args[0]` AND BUZ_FOLIO env. **Dual-channel.**
- Internal callers: pass `$1` (matches handler pattern).
- 14 BBAAs handler reverts + dispatcher rewrite + spec sweeps.

**Rely-on-plumbing (the 3-line nudge)**:
- Read patterns: **everything reads BUZ_FOLIO**. Single contract across cli.sh furnishes, handlers, BBAAs and pre-BBAAs alike.
- Write patterns: **bash's `VAR=value <command>` idiom**, used at two scopes:
  - Dispatcher: `BUZ_FOLIO="${z_folio}" exec <module> <command>` (process scope)
  - Internal callers: `BUZ_FOLIO="${vessel}" rbfd_kludge` (function-call scope)
  - Same primitive. Different scope. No new mechanism.
- 3 lines edited in `rbob_bottle.sh`. Zero dispatcher changes. Zero handler reverts.

## Why this is fewer

The dispatcher-norm proposal solves the rbob bug by **adding** a positional-arg layer alongside the existing BUZ_FOLIO layer. That's two access paths to the same value (handlers read `$1`, furnishes read BUZ_FOLIO) — a structural split that didn't exist pre-BBAAs.

The rely-on-plumbing path fixes the rbob bug by **using the existing layer correctly**. BUZ_FOLIO has been the operand carrier all along; cli.sh furnishes have read it for kindle since the older pattern; rbgg / jjfp / buhw read it pre-BBAAs; BBAAs extended this to rbfd / rbfl / rbfr / rbfc. The pattern was correct; only the rbob callsites violated it by passing positionally to a target that reads from the carrier.

The bash idiom `BUZ_FOLIO="${...}" func` IS the language's primitive for "set env var for the duration of this call" — same shape as `VAR=value cmd` in shell composition, same shape as the dispatcher's `BUZ_FOLIO="${z_folio}" exec`. Not a new mechanism, not a hack — the natural fine-grained twin to the dispatcher's coarse-grained version.

## Recap on prior objection

When you said "you don't understand BUZ_FOLIO yet" earlier, I now read that as objecting to my **framing** ("BUZ_FOLIO is the universal contract") because I hadn't actually verified it was already plumbed everywhere. After the audit confirmed cli.sh furnishes plus pre-BBAAs handlers (rbgg, jjfp, buhw) all read BUZ_FOLIO uniformly, the framing earns: BUZ_FOLIO **is** the universal operand carrier — empirically, in the codebase as it stands today.

## Refined proposal

**A. Three-line nudge in `Tools/rbk/rbob_bottle.sh`**:

```bash
# rbob_kludge (line ~489):
buc_step "Kludge: ${RBRN_MONIKER} (${RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL})"
BUZ_FOLIO="${RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL}" rbfd_kludge

# rbob_kludge_sentry (line ~520):
buc_step "Kludge: ${RBRN_MONIKER} (${RBRN_SENTRY_VESSEL})"
BUZ_FOLIO="${RBRN_SENTRY_VESSEL}" rbfd_kludge

# rbob_ordain (line ~551):
buc_step "Ordain: ${RBRN_MONIKER} (${RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL})"
BUZ_FOLIO="${RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL}" rbfd_ordain
```

Delete the now-dead `local -r z_vessel_dir=...` and `test -d "${z_vessel_dir}"` lines (existence check moves into rbfd's own `zrbfc_resolve_vessel`, where it belongs — single source of truth for vessel resolution diagnostics).

**B. Strip `--force`** from `rbfl_jettison` and `rbfl_abjure` (separate concern; same notch ok). Removes:
- `local z_force="${1:-}"` (line 323, 394)
- `buc_doc_param "--force" "..."` (line 328, 399)
- `local z_skip_confirm=false` and the `if test "${z_force}" = "--force"` branch (lines 344-348, 403-407)
- The `if test "${z_skip_confirm}" = "false"` wrapping around `buc_require` (lines 357-359, 446-…)

After B, `buc_require` confirmation runs unconditionally — the single coherent path.

**Secondary fix** (separate notch): rbob_cli.sh furnish doesn't source `rbrv_regime.sh`, so `rbrv_list_capture` is missing in the failure-listing branch of `zrbfc_resolve_vessel` when invoked from the rbob process. One-line addition: `source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbrv_regime.sh"` to `zrbob_furnish`. Not strictly needed for the rerun, but improves error diagnostics if a future case trips a similar resolution-failure path.

## What this leaves intact

- BBAAs's handler-reads-BUZ_FOLIO sweep (correct direction toward uniform contract).
- The dispatcher (no changes).
- Every other handler in the BBAAs set (works correctly; no other internal callers).
- The `rbtdri_invoke_global` Rust orchestrator paths (unchanged; dispatcher contract preserved).

The rerun will tell us whether anything past kludge-tadmor surfaces. Lower-cost path; if it works, BBAAs's bigger investment was right; if more rbob-class internal-caller sites exist, the audit recipe `grep -rn "rb[fzg][a-z]_" Tools/rbk/rbob_*.sh` finds them quickly.

Awaiting guidance on A + B.

content.text:
I've thought through this carefully. My honest assessment:

## Will A solve the kludge-tadmor blocker?

**Yes, with high confidence.** Tracing the failure mechanically against the proposed edit:

**Today's broken path:**
1. `rbw-cKS tadmor` → dispatcher sets BUZ_FOLIO="tadmor" → exec rbob_cli.sh
2. rbob furnish kindles RBRN_*; RBRN_SENTRY_VESSEL="rbev-sentry-deb-tether"
3. Calls `rbob_kludge_sentry`
4. Calls `rbfd_kludge "${z_vessel_dir}"` (positional, ignored)
5. rbfd_kludge reads BUZ_FOLIO="tadmor" (inherited, nameplate moniker)
6. zrbfc_resolve_vessel("tadmor") → no path "tadmor", no path "rbev-vessels/tadmor" → listing branch → `rbrv_list_capture` not in scope → die

**Post-A path:**
1-3 same.
4. `BUZ_FOLIO="${RBRN_SENTRY_VESSEL}" rbfd_kludge` — assignment-prefix overrides BUZ_FOLIO to "rbev-sentry-deb-tether" for this call only.
5. rbfd_kludge reads BUZ_FOLIO="rbev-sentry-deb-tether"
6. zrbfc_resolve_vessel("rbev-sentry-deb-tether") → sigil branch matches `${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}/rbev-sentry-deb-tether` → resolution succeeds → build proceeds

The fix is mechanically aligned with what the trace shows is broken. No speculation.

**kludge-ccyolo (case 3)** uses the same `rbtdro_kludge_nameplate` helper → same `rbob_kludge_sentry`/`rbob_kludge_bottle` → same `rbfd_kludge` path. Same fix applies. Should pass.

**Cases 4-8** (ordain-conjure, conjure-srjcl, ordain-airgap, ordain-bind, ordain-graft) bypass rbob entirely — they invoke `rbw-fO <vessel-dir>` via `rbtdro_invoke_logged` (Rust orchestrator). The dispatcher sets BUZ_FOLIO=vessel-dir; rbfd_ordain reads BUZ_FOLIO=vessel-dir. Works post-BBAAs without needing fix A. So fix A unblocks the rbob class entirely; ordain class is on a different path.

## Caveats and risks I want flagged

**1. The BBAAs "5/8 PASSED" narrative is unclear.** BBAAs's commit (`7167b795`) claims its post-sweep empirical run got 5/8 with ordain-airgap as the failure — meaning kludge-tadmor allegedly passed in that run. Yet today the same code under what should be similar state fails kludge-tadmor with a mechanically clear bug that BBAAs didn't fix. Possibilities: BBAAs's commit message was aspirational/partial; run state differed (stale resolved-vessel file? differently-populated nameplate?); or the message was a misreport. I don't have a clean explanation. I trust the trace from today's run as ground truth.

**2. Cases past kludge-ccyolo may hit other bugs.** Fixture is StateProgressing → fail-fast on first failure. If ordain-conjure trips a state-dependent issue, we surface it as the next iteration cycle. Per docket: each case failure → diagnose → recommend → await guidance. Plan one case at a time.

**3. The `rbrv_list_capture` not-in-scope bug becomes silent under fix A** — resolution succeeds, listing branch not entered. But it remains latent: any future failure path entering the listing branch from an rbob-process would surface "command not found" again, hiding the real diagnosis under a confusing error. Worth a follow-up notch to `source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbrv_regime.sh"` in `zrbob_furnish` (one line in `rbob_cli.sh`). Not blocking the rerun.

**4. B (--force strip) is independent of the blocker.** rbfl_jettison/abjure aren't in the kludge path. B is code hygiene — strip an unauthorized variant — orthogonal to unblocking the empirical run.

## Advice

**Proceed with A.** It will mechanically resolve kludge-tadmor and almost certainly kludge-ccyolo. Empirical re-run will then reveal whether ordain-class cases have lurking issues (separate diagnostic if so).

**B is a clean strip but doesn't gate the rerun.** Two reasonable orderings:
- **Combined notch**: A + B together → "Three-line BUZ_FOLIO override at rbob internal callers; strip unauthorized --force flag from rbfl_jettison/abjure (origin ₢APAAT 260128-2116, never operator-authorized, not load-bearing)." One commit, both addressed, rerun verifies A.
- **Sequential notches**: A first → rerun → confirm fix → B as separate hygiene notch. Clean attribution, easier to bisect if anything goes sideways.

I'd lean **combined notch** since both edits are tiny and orthogonal; rerun directly tests A; no realistic interference between them.

**The follow-up `rbrv_list_capture` source-line fix** I'd defer to a separate notch (it's diagnostic-quality improvement, not bug-fix, and slightly broadens scope from "fix the blocker" to "improve all rbob failure paths").

Ready to implement A + B as a combined notch when you give the go-ahead.

######################################################################
# Kludge — build bottle vessel locally and drive hallmark into nameplate

rbob_kludge() {
  zrbob_sentinel
  zrbfc_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "Build bottle vessel locally and drive hallmark into nameplate"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  buc_step "Kludge: ${RBRN_MONIKER} (${RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL})"

  # Resolve vessel from nameplate config
  local -r z_vessel_dir="${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}/${RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL}"
  test -d "${z_vessel_dir}" || buc_die "Bottle vessel directory not found: ${z_vessel_dir}"

  # Delegate build to foundry kludge
  rbfd_kludge "${z_vessel_dir}"

  # Read hallmark from fact file
  local z_hallmark=""
  z_hallmark=$(<"${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/${RBF_FACT_HALLMARK}") \
    || buc_die "Failed to read hallmark from kludge output"
  test -n "${z_hallmark}" || buc_die "Empty hallmark from kludge output"

  # Drive hallmark into nameplate
  zrbob_drive_hallmark "${ZRBOB_ENV_RBRN}" "RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK" "${z_hallmark}"

  buc_success "Kludge installed: ${z_hallmark} → ${RBRN_MONIKER}"
}

######################################################################
# Kludge Sentry — build sentry vessel locally and drive hallmark into nameplate

rbob_kludge_sentry() {
  zrbob_sentinel
  zrbfc_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "Build sentry vessel locally and drive hallmark into nameplate"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  buc_step "Kludge: ${RBRN_MONIKER} (${RBRN_SENTRY_VESSEL})"

  # Resolve vessel from nameplate config
  local -r z_vessel_dir="${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}/${RBRN_SENTRY_VESSEL}"
  test -d "${z_vessel_dir}" || buc_die "Sentry vessel directory not found: ${z_vessel_dir}"

  # Delegate build to foundry kludge
  rbfd_kludge "${z_vessel_dir}"

  # Read hallmark from fact file
  local z_hallmark=""
  z_hallmark=$(<"${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/${RBF_FACT_HALLMARK}") \
    || buc_die "Failed to read hallmark from kludge output"
  test -n "${z_hallmark}" || buc_die "Empty hallmark from kludge output"

  # Drive hallmark into nameplate
  zrbob_drive_hallmark "${ZRBOB_ENV_RBRN}" "RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK" "${z_hallmark}"

  buc_success "Kludge installed: ${z_hallmark} → ${RBRN_MONIKER}"
}

######################################################################
# Ordain — cloud-build bottle vessel and drive hallmark into nameplate

rbob_ordain() {
  zrbob_sentinel
  zrbfd_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "Ordain bottle vessel via cloud build and drive hallmark into nameplate"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  buc_step "Ordain: ${RBRN_MONIKER} (${RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL})"

  # Resolve vessel from nameplate config
  local -r z_vessel_dir="${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}/${RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL}"
  test -d "${z_vessel_dir}" || buc_die "Bottle vessel directory not found: ${z_vessel_dir}"

  # Delegate to foundry ordain (mode-dispatched: conjure, bind, or graft)
  rbfd_ordain "${z_vessel_dir}"

  # Read hallmark from fact file
  local z_hallmark=""
  z_hallmark=$(<"${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/${RBF_FACT_HALLMARK}") \
    || buc_die "Failed to read hallmark from ordain output"
  test -n "${z_hallmark}" || buc_die "Empty hallmark from ordain output"

  # Drive hallmark into nameplate
  zrbob_drive_hallmark "${ZRBOB_ENV_RBRN}" "RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK" "${z_hallmark}"

  buc_success "Ordain installed: ${z_hallmark} → ${RBRN_MONIKER}"
}

414	  # Each immediate child of the subtree is one ark (image, vouch, pouch,

416	  # hardcoded suffix list naturally tolerates graft's missing pouch.

429	  local -r z_pkg_file="${ZRBFL_DELETE_PREFIX}packages.txt"

432	    .packages[]?.name
433	    | sub("^.*/packages/"; "")
434	    | gsub("%2F"; "/")

454	  fi

459	  local z_pkg_path=""

rbfl_jettison() {
  zrbfl_sentinel

  local z_locator="${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"
  local z_force="${1:-}"

  # Documentation block
  buc_doc_brief "Jettison an image tag from the registry by locator"
  buc_doc_param "locator" "Image locator in package-path:tag format (e.g. rbi_hm/H/image:H)"
  buc_doc_param "--force" "Optional: skip confirmation prompt"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  # Validate locator parameter
  test -n "${z_locator}" || buc_die "Locator parameter required (package-path:tag)"

  # Parse locator into package path and tag
  case "${z_locator}" in
    *:*) : ;;
    *)   buc_die "Invalid locator format. Expected package-path:tag" ;;
  esac
  local z_pkg_path="${z_locator%:*}"
  local z_tag="${z_locator##*:}"
  test -n "${z_pkg_path}" || buc_die "Package path is empty in locator"
  test -n "${z_tag}" || buc_die "Tag is empty in locator"

  # Check for --force flag
  local z_skip_confirm=false
  if test "${z_force}" = "--force"; then
    z_skip_confirm=true
  fi

  buc_step "Authenticating as Director"

  # Get OAuth token using Director credentials
  local z_token
  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}") || buc_die "Failed to get OAuth token"

  # Confirm jettison unless --force
  if test "${z_skip_confirm}" = "false"; then
    buc_require "Will jettison: ${z_locator}" "yes"
  fi

  buc_step "Jettisoning: ${z_locator}"

  # Jettison by tag reference
  local z_status_file="${ZRBFL_DELETE_PREFIX}status.txt"
  local z_response_file="${ZRBFL_DELETE_PREFIX}response.json"

  curl -X DELETE -s                                   \
    --connect-timeout "${RBCC_CURL_CONNECT_TIMEOUT_SEC}" \
    --max-time "${RBCC_CURL_MAX_TIME_SEC}"             \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer ${z_token}"             \
    -w "%{http_code}"                                 \
    -o "${z_response_file}"                           \
    "${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_API_BASE}/${z_pkg_path}/manifests/${z_tag}" \
    > "${z_status_file}" || buc_die "DELETE request failed"

  local z_http_code
  z_http_code=$(<"${z_status_file}")
  test -n "${z_http_code}" || buc_die "HTTP status code is empty"

  if test "${z_http_code}" != "202" && test "${z_http_code}" != "204"; then
    local z_body="empty"
    if test -f "${z_response_file}"; then z_body=$(<"${z_response_file}"); fi
    buc_warn "Response body: ${z_body}"
    buc_die "Jettison failed with HTTP ${z_http_code}"
  fi

  buc_success "Jettisoned or nonexistent: ${z_locator}"
}

rbfl_abjure() {
  zrbfl_sentinel

  local z_hallmark="${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"
  local z_force="${1:-}"

  # Documentation block
  buc_doc_brief "Abjure a hallmark — delete all GAR packages under rbi_hm/<hallmark>/"
  buc_doc_param "hallmark" "Full hallmark (e.g., c260305133650-r260305160530)"
  buc_doc_param "--force" "Optional: skip confirmation prompt"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${z_hallmark}" || buc_die "Hallmark parameter required"

  local z_skip_confirm=false
  if test "${z_force}" = "--force"; then
    z_skip_confirm=true
  fi

  buc_step "Authenticating as Director"
  local z_token
  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}") || buc_die "Failed to get OAuth token"

  # Enumerate packages under rbi_hm/<hallmark>/ via GAR REST API.
  # Each immediate child of the subtree is one ark (image, vouch, pouch,
  # about, attest, diags). Iterating discovered children rather than a
  # hardcoded suffix list naturally tolerates graft's missing pouch.
  local -r z_subtree="${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/${z_hallmark}/"
  buc_step "Enumerating packages under ${z_subtree}"

  local -r z_list_url="${ZRBFC_GAR_API_BASE}/${ZRBFC_GAR_PACKAGE_BASE}/packages?pageSize=1000"
  local -r z_list_infix="rbfl_abjure_list"

  rbgu_http_json "GET" "${z_list_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_list_infix}"
  rbgu_http_require_ok "List packages for abjure" "${z_list_infix}"

  # GAR returns package names URL-encoded in the resource name (slashes as
  # %2F); decode and prefix-match to the hallmark subtree.
  local -r z_resp_file="${ZRBGU_PREFIX}${z_list_infix}${ZRBGU_POSTFIX_JSON}"
  local -r z_pkg_file="${ZRBFL_DELETE_PREFIX}packages.txt"

  jq -r --arg subtree "${z_subtree}" '
    .packages[]?.name
    | sub("^.*/packages/"; "")
    | gsub("%2F"; "/")
    | select(startswith($subtree))
  ' "${z_resp_file}" > "${z_pkg_file}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to extract package names for hallmark subtree"

  if ! test -s "${z_pkg_file}"; then
    buc_die "No packages found under ${z_subtree} — hallmark not present in registry"
  fi

  local z_count
  z_count=$(wc -l < "${z_pkg_file}" | tr -d ' ')

  # Confirm abjuration unless --force
  if test "${z_skip_confirm}" = "false"; then
    local z_confirm_msg="Will abjure ${z_count} packages under ${z_subtree}:"
    local z_pkg_path=""
    while IFS= read -r z_pkg_path || test -n "${z_pkg_path}"; do
      z_confirm_msg="${z_confirm_msg}\n  - ${z_pkg_path}"
    done < "${z_pkg_file}"
    buc_require "${z_confirm_msg}" "yes"
  fi

  # Delete each package via GAR REST API.
  # DELETE returns a long-running operation; trust 200 as accepted (matches
  # the prior fire-and-forget semantics with 202/204 on the v2 manifest API).
  local z_pkg_path=""
  local z_del_idx=0
  while IFS= read -r z_pkg_path || test -n "${z_pkg_path}"; do
    buc_step "Deleting package: ${z_pkg_path}"

    local z_pkg_encoded="${z_pkg_path//\//%2F}"
    local z_del_url="${ZRBFC_GAR_API_BASE}/${ZRBFC_GAR_PACKAGE_BASE}/packages/${z_pkg_encoded}"
    local z_del_infix="rbfl_abjure_del_${z_del_idx}"

    rbgu_http_json "DELETE" "${z_del_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_del_infix}"
    rbgu_http_require_ok "Delete package ${z_pkg_path}" "${z_del_infix}"

    buc_info "Deleted: ${z_pkg_path}"
    z_del_idx=$((z_del_idx + 1))
  done < "${z_pkg_file}"

  echo ""
  buc_success "Hallmark abjured: ${z_hallmark} (${z_count} packages)"
}

rbfl_tally() {
  zrbfl_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "Tally hallmarks with health status (vouched / pending / incomplete)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  buc_step "Authenticating as Retriever"
  test -f "${RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE}" \
    || buc_die "Retriever credential not found: ${RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE}"
  local z_token=""

content.text: Now stripping --force from rbfl_jettison and rbfl_abjure:

input.old_string:
rbfl_jettison() {
  zrbfl_sentinel

  local z_locator="${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"
  local z_force="${1:-}"

  # Documentation block
  buc_doc_brief "Jettison an image tag from the registry by locator"
  buc_doc_param "locator" "Image locator in package-path:tag format (e.g. rbi_hm/H/image:H)"
  buc_doc_param "--force" "Optional: skip confirmation prompt"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  # Validate locator parameter
  test -n "${z_locator}" || buc_die "Locator parameter required (package-path:tag)"

  # Parse locator into package path and tag
  case "${z_locator}" in
    *:*) : ;;
    *)   buc_die "Invalid locator format. Expected package-path:tag" ;;
  esac
  local z_pkg_path="${z_locator%:*}"
  local z_tag="${z_locator##*:}"
  test -n "${z_pkg_path}" || buc_die "Package path is empty in locator"
  test -n "${z_tag}" || buc_die "Tag is empty in locator"

  # Check for --force flag
  local z_skip_confirm=false
  if test "${z_force}" = "--force"; then
    z_skip_confirm=true
  fi

  buc_step "Authenticating as Director"

  # Get OAuth token using Director credentials
  local z_token
  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}") || buc_die "Failed to get OAuth token"

  # Confirm jettison unless --force
  if test "${z_skip_confirm}" = "false"; then
    buc_require "Will jettison: ${z_locator}" "yes"
  fi

  buc_step "Jettisoning: ${z_locator}"

rbfl_jettison() {
  zrbfl_sentinel

  local z_locator="${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"

  # Documentation block
  buc_doc_brief "Jettison an image tag from the registry by locator"
  buc_doc_param "locator" "Image locator in package-path:tag format (e.g. rbi_hm/H/image:H)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  # Validate locator parameter
  test -n "${z_locator}" || buc_die "Locator parameter required (package-path:tag)"

  # Parse locator into package path and tag
  case "${z_locator}" in
    *:*) : ;;
    *)   buc_die "Invalid locator format. Expected package-path:tag" ;;
  esac
  local z_pkg_path="${z_locator%:*}"
  local z_tag="${z_locator##*:}"
  test -n "${z_pkg_path}" || buc_die "Package path is empty in locator"
  test -n "${z_tag}" || buc_die "Tag is empty in locator"

  buc_step "Authenticating as Director"

  # Get OAuth token using Director credentials
  local z_token
  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}") || buc_die "Failed to get OAuth token"

  buc_require "Will jettison: ${z_locator}" "yes"

  buc_step "Jettisoning: ${z_locator}"

  # Jettison by tag reference
  local z_status_file="${ZRBFL_DELETE_PREFIX}status.txt"
  local z_response_file="${ZRBFL_DELETE_PREFIX}response.json"

  curl -X DELETE -s                                   \
    --connect-timeout "${RBCC_CURL_CONNECT_TIMEOUT_SEC}" \
    --max-time "${RBCC_CURL_MAX_TIME_SEC}"             \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer ${z_token}"             \
    -w "%{http_code}"                                 \
    -o "${z_response_file}"                           \
    "${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_API_BASE}/${z_pkg_path}/manifests/${z_tag}" \
    > "${z_status_file}" || buc_die "DELETE request failed"

  local z_http_code
  z_http_code=$(<"${z_status_file}")
  test -n "${z_http_code}" || buc_die "HTTP status code is empty"

  if test "${z_http_code}" != "202" && test "${z_http_code}" != "204"; then
    local z_body="empty"
    if test -f "${z_response_file}"; then z_body=$(<"${z_response_file}"); fi
    buc_warn "Response body: ${z_body}"
    buc_die "Jettison failed with HTTP ${z_http_code}"
  fi

  buc_success "Jettisoned or nonexistent: ${z_locator}"
}

rbfl_abjure() {
  zrbfl_sentinel

  local z_hallmark="${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"

  # Documentation block
  buc_doc_brief "Abjure a hallmark — delete all GAR packages under rbi_hm/<hallmark>/"
  buc_doc_param "hallmark" "Full hallmark (e.g., c260305133650-r260305160530)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${z_hallmark}" || buc_die "Hallmark parameter required"

  buc_step "Authenticating as Director"
  local z_token
  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}") || buc_die "Failed to get OAuth token"

  # Enumerate packages under rbi_hm/<hallmark>/ via GAR REST API.
  # Each immediate child of the subtree is one ark (image, vouch, pouch,
  # about, attest, diags). Iterating discovered children rather than a
  # hardcoded suffix list naturally tolerates graft's missing pouch.
  local -r z_subtree="${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/${z_hallmark}/"
  buc_step "Enumerating packages under ${z_subtree}"

  local -r z_list_url="${ZRBFC_GAR_API_BASE}/${ZRBFC_GAR_PACKAGE_BASE}/packages?pageSize=1000"
  local -r z_list_infix="rbfl_abjure_list"

  rbgu_http_json "GET" "${z_list_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_list_infix}"
  rbgu_http_require_ok "List packages for abjure" "${z_list_infix}"

  # GAR returns package names URL-encoded in the resource name (slashes as
  # %2F); decode and prefix-match to the hallmark subtree.
  local -r z_resp_file="${ZRBGU_PREFIX}${z_list_infix}${ZRBGU_POSTFIX_JSON}"
  local -r z_pkg_file="${ZRBFL_DELETE_PREFIX}packages.txt"

  jq -r --arg subtree "${z_subtree}" '
    .packages[]?.name
    | sub("^.*/packages/"; "")
    | gsub("%2F"; "/")
    | select(startswith($subtree))
  ' "${z_resp_file}" > "${z_pkg_file}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to extract package names for hallmark subtree"

  if ! test -s "${z_pkg_file}"; then
    buc_die "No packages found under ${z_subtree} — hallmark not present in registry"
  fi

  local z_count
  z_count=$(wc -l < "${z_pkg_file}" | tr -d ' ')

  local z_confirm_msg="Will abjure ${z_count} packages under ${z_subtree}:"
  local z_pkg_path=""
  while IFS= read -r z_pkg_path || test -n "${z_pkg_path}"; do
    z_confirm_msg="${z_confirm_msg}\n  - ${z_pkg_path}"
  done < "${z_pkg_file}"
  buc_require "${z_confirm_msg}" "yes"

  # Delete each package via GAR REST API.
  # DELETE returns a long-running operation; trust 200 as accepted (matches
  # the prior fire-and-forget semantics with 202/204 on the v2 manifest API).
  local z_pkg_path=""
  local z_del_idx=0
  while IFS= read -r z_pkg_path || test -n "${z_pkg_path}"; do
    buc_step "Deleting package: ${z_pkg_path}"

    local z_pkg_encoded="${z_pkg_path//\//%2F}"
    local z_del_url="${ZRBFC_GAR_API_BASE}/${ZRBFC_GAR_PACKAGE_BASE}/packages/${z_pkg_encoded}"
    local z_del_infix="rbfl_abjure_del_${z_del_idx}"

    rbgu_http_json "DELETE" "${z_del_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_del_infix}"
    rbgu_http_require_ok "Delete package ${z_pkg_path}" "${z_del_infix}"

    buc_info "Deleted: ${z_pkg_path}"
    z_del_idx=$((z_del_idx + 1))
  done < "${z_pkg_file}"

  echo ""
  buc_success "Hallmark abjured: ${z_hallmark} (${z_count} packages)"
}

rbfl_tally() {
  zrbfl_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "Tally hallmarks with health status (vouched / pending / incomplete)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  buc_step "Authenticating as Retriever"

buc_require() {
  local z_prompt="$1"
  local z_required_value="$2"

  if test "${BURE_CONFIRM:-}" = "skip"; then
    buc_step "Confirm: (skipped — BURE_CONFIRM=skip)"
    return 0
  fi

  test -z "${BURE_CONFIRM:-}" || buc_die "BURE_CONFIRM must be 'skip' or unset, got '${BURE_CONFIRM}'"

  sleep 1
  printf '%b\n' "${BUC_yellow}${z_prompt}${BUC_reset}" >/dev/tty
  printf 'Type %s to confirm: ' "${z_required_value}" >/dev/tty
  local z_input
  read -r z_input </dev/tty
  test "${z_input}" = "${z_required_value}" || buc_die "Confirmation failed — expected '${z_required_value}', got '${z_input}'"
}

buc_execute() {
  set -e
  local prefix="$1"
  local title="$2"
  local env_func="$3"
  local command="${4:-}"
  shift 3; test -z "${command}" || shift

  export BUC_VERBOSE="${BUC_VERBOSE:-0}"

  # Enable bash trace to stderr if BUC_VERBOSE is 3 or higher and bash >= 4.1
  if test "${BUC_VERBOSE}" -ge 3; then
    if test "${BASH_VERSINFO[0]}" -gt 4 || { test "${BASH_VERSINFO[0]}" -eq 4 && test "${BASH_VERSINFO[1]}" -ge 1; }; then
      export PS4='+ ${BASH_SOURCE##*/}:${LINENO}: '
      export BASH_XTRACEFD=2

(A) Three-line nudge in rbob_bottle.sh: at the three internal callsites (rbob_kludge:503, rbob_kludge_sentry:534, rbob_ordain:565) replace `rbfd_kludge "${z_vessel_dir}"` with `BUZ_FOLIO="${RBRN_*_VESSEL}" rbfd_kludge` (and the ordain analog). Drops the now-dead `local -r z_vessel_dir="${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}/${RBRN_*_VESSEL}"` plus its `test -d` guard at each site (existence check moves into rbfd's own `zrbfc_resolve_vessel`, single source of truth for vessel resolution diagnostics). Solves kludge-tadmor blocker observed in today's empirical run: trace `Tools/rbk/rbfc_FoundryCore.sh: line 185: rbrv_list_capture: command not found` was symptom of `zrbfc_resolve_vessel(BUZ_FOLIO="tadmor")` falling through to listing branch because `tadmor` is the nameplate moniker (inherited from rbw-cKS dispatch into rbob_cli.sh process), not a vessel sigil — rbfd_kludge expected sigil/path. Bash assignment-prefix `VAR=value func` is the language's primitive for scoped env override and is symmetric with the dispatcher's `BUZ_FOLIO="${z_folio}" exec` (buz_zipper.sh:280) — same primitive at finer scope. Preserves BBAAs's universal-BUZ_FOLIO-contract design intent (handlers read BUZ_FOLIO uniformly across all rb*_, jjfp_, buhw_, rbgg_ modules; cli.sh furnishes also read BUZ_FOLIO for kindle); the rbob → rbfd internal calls just needed to honor the contract by setting BUZ_FOLIO to rbob's natural-derivative (RBRN_*_VESSEL sigil) for the inner call. After this patch, BUZ_FOLIO usage is uniform: dispatcher writes once via exec, callers may scope-override via assignment-prefix, all consumers read. Zero handler/dispatcher edits needed. (B) Strip --force flag from rbfl_jettison and rbfl_abjure plus their RBSIJ/RBSAA specs and 6 Rust orchestrator callsites in rbtdrc_crucible.rs — origin trace: docket revision 260128-2116 for ₢APAAT (silks `add-image-delete-by-ark`) introduced 'Confirm before deletion or require --force flag' as freelance docket text never operator-authorized; subsequent reslating preserved it; alignment commit 525b7fac (`:A:` Feb 9 2026) pulled `(add confirmation/force)` into the spec; bd344f92 (Haiku sub-agent, same day) implemented z_force/z_skip_confirm/--force doc-param mechanically; 388dd454 carried it through the rbfl_FoundryLedger.sh extraction; 7167b795 (BBAAs) included `${2:-}` → `${1:-}` shift specifically because of --force. Removed: `local z_force="${1:-}"`, `buc_doc_param "--force" "..."`, `local z_skip_confirm=false` + `if test "${z_force}" = "--force"` branch, and the `if test "${z_skip_confirm}" = "false"` wrapping around `buc_require` — `buc_require` runs unconditionally (single coherent path). The codebase already had a non-load-bearing-correct skip mechanism (`BURE_CONFIRM=skip` env var checked at buc_require:335-338, buc_command.sh) — --force was a redundant duplicate variant. Updated all 6 Rust orchestrator callsites in rbtdrc_crucible.rs (lines 2603, 2688, 2720, 2820, 2948, 3003) from `&[&hallmark, "--force"], &[]` to `&[&hallmark], &[("BURE_CONFIRM", "skip")]` — fixtures continue to auto-confirm via the existing env-var bypass; --force literal disappears from the entire rbk tree (verified via grep). Spec edits: RBSIJ-image_jettison.adoc:51-59 collapses 'Unless --force flag is provided' nested-step into unconditional 'Confirm Jettison' step; RBSAA-ark_abjure.adoc:84-92 same shape. Verification: bash -n green on rbob_bottle.sh and rbfl_FoundryLedger.sh; tt/rbtd-b.Build.sh green (theurge dev profile, 1.76s, no Rust warnings); tt/rbw-tf.QualifyFast.sh green (tabtargets, RBW colophon registrations, generated context freshness all OK); tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh 25/26 with rbtdrf_rv_rbrv_all_vessels pre-existing fail unchanged from BBAA3/BBAA4/BBAAs baseline (carrying-forward marshal-zero validator surfacing) — no new failures introduced. Empirical re-run of onboarding-sequence pending operator go-ahead per docket iteration loop. Deferred follow-ups (separate notches when relevant): (i) source rbrv_regime.sh in zrbob_furnish to make rbrv_list_capture available in failure-listing branches reached from rbob processes (currently latent, surfaces only on resolution-failure paths and obscures real diagnostics); (ii) audit other Rust orchestrator/handbook callers passing positional args to BUZ_FOLIO-reading handlers — discovery recipe `grep -rn "rbtdri_invoke_global\|rbtdri_invoke_global_imprint" Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/` cross-referenced against zipper channel column for any handler with internal bash callers in rb*.sh modules. Caveat: scoped to the empirical failure trace and the unauthorized-flag scrub; if any handler outside this bug class has a similar internal-caller mismatch, will surface on the next empirical run as a new failure to diagnose.

params.intent: RCG named-string repair on the prior notch's BURE_CONFIRM bypass introduction. Operator flagged the 6 sites in rbtdrc_crucible.rs that read `&[("BURE_CONFIRM", "skip")]` as RCG-violating magic strings (per Tools/vok/vov_veiled/RCG-RustCodingGuide.md the codebase requires named string constants for env-var keys and protocol values). Prior precedent already existed: rbtdrp_pristine.rs:106-107 had module-private `const RBTDRP_BURE_CONFIRM_KEY: &str = "BURE_CONFIRM"` + `const RBTDRP_BURE_CONFIRM_SKIP: &str = "skip"` consumed at line 1030 — same primitive, but module-prefixed and not shared. Adding a second module's worth of magic strings would have created a duplicate-constants smell across rbtdrp_/rbtdrc_, so consolidated by extraction: defined `pub const RBTDRI_BURE_CONFIRM_KEY: &str = "BURE_CONFIRM"` and `pub const RBTDRI_BURE_CONFIRM_SKIP: &str = "skip"` in rbtdri_invocation.rs (the natural home: rbtdri_invoke_global is the primitive that propagates env-pairs to tabtargets, and rbtdri_invocation.rs already exports RBTDRI_BURV_OUTPUT_SUBDIR and RBTDRI_IFRIT_BINARY using the same pattern). Updated rbtdrc_crucible.rs (6 sites) and rbtdrp_pristine.rs (1 site at line 1025; was line 1030 before the dead-constant deletion) to import and use the new shared constants. Removed the rbtdrp_-private duplicates (4 lines including the docstring) since the shared rbtdri_ versions now serve as the single source of truth across the rbtd crate. Verification: tt/rbtd-b.Build.sh green (0.51s, no warnings); tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh 25/26 with rbtdrf_rv_rbrv_all_vessels pre-existing failure unchanged from BBAA3/BBAA4/BBAAs/this-officium-prior-notch baseline; grep `\"BURE_CONFIRM\"\|\"skip\"` confirms no remaining magic-string sites of these values in src/. Out of scope this notch (would expand the patch surface beyond what the operator review identified): broader audit of other env-var literals in rbtd/src/ such as RBTDRI_IFRIT_BINARY-pattern strings used elsewhere, and possible pull-up of rbtdri_invocation.rs's BURV_OUTPUT_SUBDIR alongside other dispatch-side conventions (BURD_BUK_DIR, BURD_TOOLS_DIR keys consumed by spawned tabtargets) into a unified env-key constants table. Discovery recipe for residual magic strings: `grep -rn '\\"[A-Z_]\\+\\"' Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/ | grep -v '//\\|test_'` to enumerate uppercase string literals that look like env keys or protocol tokens, then evaluate per RCG whether each warrants extraction — deferred since today's empirical loop is the active priority.

content.text:
Both notches landed. Summary:

- **Notch 1** (`3eda4229`): A + B combined — three-line BUZ_FOLIO override at rbob internal callers; `--force` flag stripped from `rbfl_jettison`/`rbfl_abjure` + 6 Rust callsites + 2 specs. Replaced with `BURE_CONFIRM=skip` env-var bypass (the codebase's existing non-load-bearing-correct mechanism).
- **Notch 2** (`eba8f52f`): RCG magic-string repair you flagged. Defined `pub const RBTDRI_BURE_CONFIRM_KEY` / `RBTDRI_BURE_CONFIRM_SKIP` in `rbtdri_invocation.rs` (the natural home, alongside `RBTDRI_BURV_OUTPUT_SUBDIR`); consolidated `rbtdrp_pristine.rs`'s module-private duplicates onto the shared constants; updated all 7 callsites across `rbtdrc_crucible.rs` and `rbtdrp_pristine.rs`.

Build green; fast suite 25/26 (pre-existing baseline). Ready for empirical re-run of `onboarding-sequence` on your go-ahead — A should mechanically clear `kludge-tadmor` and `kludge-ccyolo`; cases 4-8 will surface as the next iteration if anything lurks past the kludge bugs.
